title: FAQ
text:
Applying
FAQ
Applying to HKU
Programme Listing
Admission Requirements
Application Procedures
Application Fees
FAQ
Programme and Study
1. When will the application information be available?
Please note that application information for admission to most taught postgraduate programmes will usually be available from October/November onwards every year. Some postgraduate programmes will be open for application earlier. Please stay tune to the announcement of individual faculties/departments.
2. Where can I obtain the application information?
Please visit our
website
for information about admission requirements, programme list, programme information, syllabus for each programme, application procedure, supporting document etc.
3. Where can I obtain information on PhD/MPhil programmes?
Please contact the Graduate School for inquiries about Research Postgraduate Programmes.
Application

title: FAQ
text:
D/MPhil programmes?
Please contact the Graduate School for inquiries about Research Postgraduate Programmes.
Application
1. How can I submit my application?
You can apply for our taught postgraduate programmes via the online application system by clicking the "Apply Now" button in the programme page. If you cannot click the button, that means application is not open yet. Please wait until the Department/Faculty concerned announces the latest programme details, which are usually be available from October/November onwards.
2. Can I apply to more than one taught postgraduate programme at HKU?
Yes, it is possible for you to apply to multiple programmes at HKU. You can apply under different usernames on the application system and pay the application fee (HK$600) for each programme that you wish to apply to.
You can only enrol in one programme, even if you receive offers from other programmes.

title: FAQ
text:
gramme that you wish to apply to.
You can only enrol in one programme, even if you receive offers from other programmes.
3. Can I apply for admission if i am still in my last year of study?
You do not need to have graduated from your bachelor's degree programme at the time of application. However, you must obtain a bachelor's degree on or before August 31 of your admission year.
4. Is concurrent registration acceptable?
Applicants are required to provide proof of withdrawal from any other programme of study before confirming an offer. A breach of this regulation may result in discontinuation of studies at HKU.
5. Am I a local or a non-local applicant?
For definition of local students, please visit Joint University Programmes Admissions System (JUPAS) website
here
.
6. How can I retrieve my application?
If you have logged out your account before submission, please click
here

title: FAQ
text:
te
here
.
6. How can I retrieve my application?
If you have logged out your account before submission, please click
here
to retrieve and re-login to your account.
7. Why can't log in to my application account?
An application account is only valid for 4 weeks, or up until the application deadline, whichever is earlier. You cannot log into your application account after this period.
8. Whom should I reach out to for guidance when completing the online application form?
For any assistance or inquiries regarding the completion of the online application form, please contact the programme office.
English Language Requirement
1. If the medium of instruction for my degree is in English, do I still need to take the English Proficiency test?

title: FAQ
text:
ement
1. If the medium of instruction for my degree is in English, do I still need to take the English Proficiency test?
You are not required to take the English proficiency test if you obtained a master's/bachelor's degree from an institution where the medium of instruction was English. Please check with the relevant Faculty to determine if higher English competency is required for certain programmes.
Fee, Payment & Refund
1. Can my application fee be waived?
The application fee cannot be waived. However, you may wish to check with the programme office for scholarships which cover the application fee.
2. What can I do if I have problems in settling the application fee on the online application system?
If you encounter problems with the online payment, please try the following:

title: FAQ
text:
ation fee on the online application system?
If you encounter problems with the online payment, please try the following:
Use browsers such as, Internet Explorer (IE) or Edge instead of Chrome or Safari and clear all the temporary files and cookies before submitting the payment.
Ensure not to open different browsers and/or log in to different application accounts at the same time.
Contact the credit card issuing bank to check if you need to obtain authorization from the bank before making an online transaction.
Attempt to use another credit card.
3. How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted the application?
Upon successful payment of the application fee and submission of an application, the applicant will receive an application number and an email notification within 48 hours.

title: FAQ
text:
ubmission of an application, the applicant will receive an application number and an email notification within 48 hours.
4. What should I do if the application fee has been deducted but the application is not submitted successfully?
If the application fee has been deducted but the system indicates that the application was not submitted successfully, there are a few steps you can take to resolve the issue. First, please note that an application number will only be generated if the application is submitted successfully, and the payment will be deducted based on that application number.
To resolve the issue, please follow these steps:
1. Re-login to the system and try submitting the payment again. Continue doing so until you receive an application number from the system.
2. Before submitting the payment, it is advisable to remove all temporary files and cookies from your browser.

title: FAQ
text:
e system.
2. Before submitting the payment, it is advisable to remove all temporary files and cookies from your browser.
3. Ensure that you do not open different browsers or log in to different application accounts (if you have any) simultaneously.
Please keep in mind that we can only charge once per application number. The funds for both successful and unsuccessful attempts at application fee payments are held by the card-issuing bank, not the University. The bank will release the balance held for the unsuccessful payment attempt(s) at a later time, but the duration of these holds depends on the policy of the card-issuing bank.
5. If I cancel/withdraw my application, can I get a refund of my application fee?
The application fee is non-refundable and non-transferable once submitted.
6. Is the deposit payment refundable and transferable?

title: FAQ
text:
cation fee is non-refundable and non-transferable once submitted.
6. Is the deposit payment refundable and transferable?
Deposit is non-refundable unless you do not meet the conditions specified in the offer of acceptance. For details, please contact the relevant programme office.
After Applying
1. What should I do if I forget my username and password?
If you forget your password, click “Forget password” on the login page and reset your password.
If you forget your username, please contact the relevant faculty or programme office.
2. I submitted my application successfully but still received an account reminder email. What should I do?
This email serves as a reminder that you have created an account for online application without submission. You can let the application account expire if you no longer wish to submit another application.
3. How can I submit the supporting documents?

title: FAQ
text:
cation account expire if you no longer wish to submit another application.
3. How can I submit the supporting documents?
The ways to submit supporting documents may vary by programme. Please refer to "Supporting Documents" of relevant programmes.
4. I cannot upload my documents on the online application system. What should I do?
Please note that you can only upload your supporting documents before the application deadline or within four weeks of your account creation. If the uploading period has expired, or the file size is too large, you can contact the relevant programme office for advice.
5. What should I do if I wish to add/update my referee information?
You can email the updated referee information to respective programme office.
6. Do I need to post the original/certified true copy of the supporting documents after submitting an application?

title: FAQ
text:
fice.
6. Do I need to post the original/certified true copy of the supporting documents after submitting an application?
For most programmes, posting documents is not required at the application stage. Should an applicant be considered for admission, he/she will receive an email notification from the programme office to submit the original documents. For details, please refer to the "Supporting Documents" of relevant programmes.
7. Can I change the programme after the submission of application?
The application form once submitted is non-transferrable.
8. What should I do if I wish to change my personal information on the system?
You cannot make any changes in the application form after submission. If you need to make changes to your personal information, please send an email to the relevant programme office and provide your application number and ID/passport number for verification.

title: FAQ
text:
d an email to the relevant programme office and provide your application number and ID/passport number for verification.
9. How can I add or amend information in the application form after the submission?
You cannot make any changes in the application form after submission. To update or add additional information, please email relevant programme office directly.
10. Will I be invited to attend an admission interview or admission test?
Not all programmes require interviews or tests, please consult the relevant programme office for details.
11. When will the application result be announced? How can I check my application status?
Please consult the relevant programme office to check your application status or result.
12. How can I obtain a student visa?
Please note that the processing time for student visa applications is approximately 10-12 weeks. For details, please visit our website:

title: FAQ
text:
t the processing time for student visa applications is approximately 10-12 weeks. For details, please visit our website:
HKU Student Visa
Scholarship and Housing
1. Are there any scholarships available for my study in HKU?
For information on scholarship opportunities, you may contact the scholarship office and programme office for advice.
2. Is postgraduate housing available?
Residential places on campus for postgraduate students are limited and in high demand. To learn more about eligibility for University housing, please visit the
CEDARS website
for details and to access the online application.

THE UNIVERSITY OF HONG KONG
Guide to Online Master Registration
How to complete the online Master Registration Form
1. After you obtain your HKU P ortal account details, you should immediately login to
http://hkuportal.hku.hk, click “Self-Service” under “MyPage”, then click “Master Registration ” to
complete your online M aster Registration. If you do not complete online Master Registration, your
Student Registration Card cannot be issued and you cannot enroll on any course.
2. There are 6 pages in the Master R egistration form. Page s 1 to 5 are for data input. Page 6 is the
confirmation page. After completing each page, press “Save & Next” to go to the next pa ge. When you
have reviewed the information on Page 6, press “Submit” to confirm the data you have entered. After

title: mr_guide
text:
next pa ge. When you
have reviewed the information on Page 6, press “Submit” to confirm the data you have entered. After
pressing the “Submit” button, you will see an acknowledgement page. Logout from the system and close
all the browser windows.
3. Please take note of the following when you fill in the registration form:
(a) University Number and Degree Curriculum
These fields will be pre -entered for you on the basis of information extracted from your admission
records. No changes will be allowed.
(b) Name
You should enter your name as it appears in your HKID Card, Passport or Exit-Entry Permit for
Travelling to and from Hong Kong and Macau (往來港澳通行證). Do not enter a comma to separate
your Christian name. If you type in anything different, your student registration Card, degree diploma
and other do cuments from the University will be issued with a name different from the one shown on

title: mr_guide
text:
Card, degree diploma
and other do cuments from the University will be issued with a name different from the one shown on
your H KID Card or Passport. This may create future difficulties for you with respect to identity
verification.
(c) CC Code of your Chinese name
Enter the CC Codes of your Chinese name as indicated on your HKID Card, if applicable. Otherwise,
please leave this field blank.
After inputting the CC Codes, you should click on the ‘Display Chinese Na me’ button. If the Chinese
name displayed is incorrect, you can find other choices provided for each of the Chinese characters.
(d) HKID Number
If available, enter your Hong Kong Identity (HKID) Card number carefully.
If you still have not received your HKID Card (not even a temporary one), please leave this field blank
and fill in your Passport number or your identity card number from your home country. You should

title: mr_guide
text:
leave this field blank
and fill in your Passport number or your identity card number from your home country. You should
submit a photocopy of your HKID Card to your Faculty after it has been issued.
Chinese name (if appropriate) will not be shown on University documents if HKID Card record is
missing.
(e) Passport number
Please fill in the number of your Passport/ Exit-Entry Permit for Travelling to and from Hong Kong and
Macau and “Issue Country”. If you have entered your HKID Card number, please leave this field blank.
(f) ID number of home country
Please fill in this field if you have a national ID from Mainland China. If you have entered your HKID
number, please leave this field blank.
(g) Place of Residence/Household in Mainland China (ONLY for students from Mainland China)
If you are a student from Mainland China, you MUST fill in this field. Otherwise, leave this field blank.

title: mr_guide
text:
ainland China)
If you are a student from Mainland China, you MUST fill in this field. Otherwise, leave this field blank.
[PDF page 2]
(h) Type of Visa/Entry Permit Required to Study in Hong Kong
This section is for you to confirm your local/non-local status since application for admission. The default
status is set according to the information you provided during application for admissi on. If the status
shown is no longer accurate, please use the provided link
http://www.ase.hku.hk/doc/change_mr_design_for_non_local.pdf to download the ‘Request Form’
and update your status and contact your Faculty immediately after your Master Registration.
(i) Place of Permanent Right of Abode
Select the place where you have the legal right of permanent residence.
(j) Country of Legal Nationality
This must be as stated in the Passport you hold. SAR/BNO Passport or D.I. holders with no other valid

title: mr_guide
text:
Legal Nationality
This must be as stated in the Passport you hold. SAR/BNO Passport or D.I. holders with no other valid
Passport of another country should select “Hong Kong (SAR) of PRC ” as their Country of Legal
Nationality. Only holders of a valid Passport issued by People’s Republic of China can select “China” as
their Country of Legal Nationality.
(k) Type of Disability
If applicable, please complete as appropriate.
(l) Contact Address (in English)
Please fill in your complete address in Hong Kong for postal purpose in the three lines provided.
(m) Home Address (in English)
Please fill in your complete home address in your home country for postal purpose in the three lines
provided. You must clearly state the Country in your address.
(n) Contact Phone number
Please enter your phone number in Hong Kong.
(o) Mobile Phone number

title: mr_guide
text:
e Country in your address.
(n) Contact Phone number
Please enter your phone number in Hong Kong.
(o) Mobile Phone number
(You may leave it blank and input it later if you do not have a HK mobile phone number yet, but you
must update it in HKU Portal once it is available)
Please enter your 8-digit mobile phone number in Hong Kong without hyphen or bracket signs (for
example, 1234XXXX). The University will from time to time call you or send you important SMS
messages via this number, e.g. about rescheduling of examinations during bad weather.
(p) Home Phone number
Please enter your home phone number in your home country.
(q) Emergency Contact
Please enter a contact person in case of an emergency occurring during your period of study in HKU.
(r) Bank Account Details
You should provide details of your account at one of the local bank s so that the University can deposit

title: mr_guide
text:
Account Details
You should provide details of your account at one of the local bank s so that the University can deposit
any scholarships and grants awarded to you.
After completing the online Master Registration Form
1. After completing the online Master Registration, you should submit your photograph and supporting
documents for production of the Student Registration Card. Please refer to the details on Registration and
Academic Induction Days -> [Select Admissions Scheme] -> [Select your curriculum].
2. Once the Master Registration page is submitted, it will no longer be available for modification. Any
subsequent changes of personal particulars after Master Registration ( except address, telephone number
and bank account details which can be amended via the HKU Portal) will have to be reported to your

title: mr_guide
text:
ess, telephone number
and bank account details which can be amended via the HKU Portal) will have to be reported to your
Faculty Office or the Academic Support and Examinations Section together with the relevant supporting
documents.

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
Offer Acceptance
Accept the Offer
I have received an admission offer online. What should I do next?
Accept the offer online by the offer acceptance deadline.
Pay the deposit to confirm your acceptance.
Follow through with other important actions as stated in the offer letter.
If you are a current/ previous student at the University, make sure you have settled all outstanding payment from your studies in order to proceed to new program registration.
How should I notify the University that I have met the offer conditions?
Submit relevant documents as proof as early as possible and no later than mid-July for Fall admission, or end December for Spring admission.
How can I pay the deposit for accepting the offer?
Deposit payment instructions will be available after you click “Accept Offer and Pay” in the Online Admission System.
Please upload the payment proof by the deadline.

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
after you click “Accept Offer and Pay” in the Online Admission System.
Please upload the payment proof by the deadline.
Supporting Documents
I have accepted the offer. What supporting documents should I submit and how?
Check the Document Checklist which will appear after you have accepted the offer online.
Refer to Document Requirements for details and tips as below:
Arrange official documents to be sent before the deadline stipulated in the offer letter/ Document Checklist.
Check the document receipt status in the Document Checklist.
What do you mean by official certified true copies?
The University may accept the following as official certified true copies only in cases where the originals of the official documents required to confirm the admission offer cannot be obtained despite all reasonable efforts or provided:

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
fficial documents required to confirm the admission offer cannot be obtained despite all reasonable efforts or provided:
(1) Copies officially imprinted, signed and certified by the issuing authority concerned, or
(2) Copies that have been certified as certified true copies by qualified professionals including lawyers, certified public accountants or notaries public. (2), however, is not applicable to qualification documents including but not limited to academic transcripts, score reports of public examinations, certification of graduation, and verification reports provided by authorities such as the CHSI.
Certified true copies documents should be sent to the HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School (FYTGS) by post. The University reserves the right to decide whether the submitted documents are acceptable.

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
uate School (FYTGS) by post. The University reserves the right to decide whether the submitted documents are acceptable.
Please refer to the FYTGS website for detailed guidelines on submitting official documents before program registration.
My official supporting document is not in English. What should I do?
You should submit the official electronic supporting document which is accompanied by officially certified English translations and is shared by the central unit of your home institution to pgdoc@ust.hk directly via designated secure websites.
The translator should sign and stamp on the translation and certify that the document is a true translation of the original.
Please visit here for details.
What are the University Codes for TOEFL, IELTS, GMAT and GRE?

Can I arrange electronic transcript for qualification verification?
Yes, the official electronic transcripts should be shared directly by the central unit of the issuing authorities.
Please request your home institution to send official electronic transcripts quoting your application number and applied program to pgdoc@ust.hk.
Please note that official electronic transcripts are acceptable only if they are sent from the issuing authorities and retrieved from designated secure websites, which the said practice has been indicating on the website of the issuing authorities.
Where should I send the supporting documents to?
Official electronic supporting documents must be sent directly by the issuing authority to pgdoc@ust.hk.

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
g documents to?
Official electronic supporting documents must be sent directly by the issuing authority to pgdoc@ust.hk.
Electronic documents which are sent by applicants or other individuals are not accepted.
Hard copies of the official documents are generally not accepted.
I have submitted my supporting documents, what’s next?
Login to the application system to check the document receipt status.
Normally it takes 7 working days (or 10 working days during the peak in June to August) to update the document receipt status after a document was received. Please also ensure that the documents have been sent to the right e-mail address.
Student Visa/ Entry Permit
Application Procedures
I have submitted my visa application online, what’s next?
Send us the required paper documents as soon as possible.

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
ures
I have submitted my visa application online, what’s next?
Send us the required paper documents as soon as possible.
Please refer to the Checklist of Documents Required for Visa/ Entry Permit Application for details.
You can check your application status in the Visa System.
How do I know the status of my student visa/ entry permit application?
Log in to the Visa System which shows the latest visa/ entry permit application status.
The University will advise you to download the e-Visa for entry to Hong Kong for study, upon the visa approval.
Approved visa/ entry permit must be activated on or before the specified date on the e-Visa.
Do I need to activate my student visa/ student status in Hong Kong?
Click here for guidelines to activate your student visa. These instructions will be sent to you together with your student visa/ entry permit. It is important that you do the steps right.

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
ructions will be sent to you together with your student visa/ entry permit. It is important that you do the steps right.
Can I apply for a dependent visa for my family member through HKUST?
You have to make your own arrangement to apply for dependent visa(s) for your family member(s).
Also, your dependents are prohibited from taking up employment in Hong Kong unless they have obtained prior permission from the Director of Immigration.
Visit here and submit ID997 to the Hong Kong Immigration Department.
For more details, check with the Hong Kong Immigration Department [phone: (852) 2824 6111; email: enquiry@immd.gov.hk].
Do I need a student visa/ entry permit?
Most non-local students are required to obtain the following for studying in Hong Kong:
student visa (if you are from an overseas country)
entry permit (if you are from Macao, Taiwan or Mainland China)
Please visit here for details.

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
from an overseas country)
entry permit (if you are from Macao, Taiwan or Mainland China)
Please visit here for details.
How should I apply for the student visa/ entry permit?
You can apply through HKUST by lodging your application via HKUST Visa System. The University will act as your sponsor.
Please visit here for the application timeline and procedures.
For students from Taiwan, Macao or an overseas country, you may choose to file an application on your own (e.g. through the consulate general of your country). You will need to find a sponsor and make your own arrangement to collect the visa label.
When should I apply for the student visa/ entry permit?
Application period:
Fall admission: March or soonest possible
Spring admission: mid-October or soonest possible

title: FAQ | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
permit?
Application period:
Fall admission: March or soonest possible
Spring admission: mid-October or soonest possible
For exchange program, PG visiting student program and visiting internship: apply right after you have accepted the admission offer
For DBA, EMBA, MBA, HKUST-NYU MSc in Global Finance: respective program offices will advise you of the details separately
It is important to submit your application early to ensure that you can obtain the visa/entry permit in time for program commencement.
Please visit here for a rough application timeline based on our past experience, for reference purpose only. The processing and approval processes rest with the Hong Kong Immigration Department and situation may vary from case to case.

Please make use of the mailing cover when sending the supporting documents by courier to HKUST.
What amount should I put as financial proof?
It is important to show that you are well covered financially during your studies at HKUST (see sample application form).
The Hong Kong Immigration Department requires documentary proof showing your financial standing, e.g. a photocopy of latest bank statement or passbook.
If you are awarded a scholarship or a Postgraduate Studentship, please indicate the monthly amount in the application form.

Congratulations on receiving your offer! Accept the offer now and get prepared for your study journey at HKUST!
Step 1 : Accept the Admission Offer Online
Confirm the offer and pay the deposit to confirm your acceptance.
Tip: Offers are only considered accepted when both online acceptance and deposit payment are completed.
Step 2 : Submit Student Visa Application (for Non-Local Students)
Apply for the Student Visa/ Entry Permit prior to program commencement. Most non-local students are required to obtain a student visa/ entry permit for studying in Hong Kong.
Learn more
Tip: Visa extension is not applicable for current HKUST final year students seeking admission to a new study program at HKUST.

title: Accepting an Offer | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
isa extension is not applicable for current HKUST final year students seeking admission to a new study program at HKUST.
Step 3 : Submit Documents to Verify Your Qualification
Arrange official transcripts and other necessary documentation to be sent directly by the issuing authorities by the deadline stipulated in the offer letter/ Document Checklist.
Learn more
Step 4 : Register at HKUST
Check out the invitation for registration and register at HKUST according to the instructions. Students are expected to register at HKUST in mid-August for Fall entry or mid-January for Spring entry.
In addition, if you are a current/ previous student at HKUST, please ensure that you have settled all outstanding payment from your studies in order to proceed to new program registration.

Who is a Non-Local Student?
A student holding one of the following documents is considered as a non-local student:
Student visa/ entry permit; or

; or
Dependent visa/ entry permit
, who were 18 years old or above when they were issued with such visa/ entry permit by the Director of Immigration.
For more details on definition of non-local students, please visit

.
A non-local student who holds student visa/entry permit is not allowed to pursue part-time research postgraduate studies (MPhil/PhD) in Hong Kong.

title: Applying for Student Visa | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
part-time research postgraduate studies (MPhil/PhD) in Hong Kong.
Image
Text Area
Who Needs Student Visa/ Entry Permit?
Most non-local students are required to obtain a student visa (or entry permit for students from the Mainland of China, Macao and Taiwan) for studying in Hong Kong. If you are from the Mainland of China, you must apply for an entry permit via HKUST. If you are from Taiwan, Macao or an overseas country, it is the simplest and most straightforward for you to apply for a student visa/ entry permit via HKUST.
For students currently studying in Hong Kong (including HKUST), you will have to submit an application for the new program of study at HKUST.
However, you don’t need the student visa/ entry permit if you have one of the following for the period of study:

title: Applying for Student Visa | HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School
text:
HKUST.
However, you don’t need the student visa/ entry permit if you have one of the following for the period of study:
(i) Right of Abode, (ii) Right to Land or (iii) “Unconditional Stay” status in Hong Kong. Check for the symbol “A”, “R” or “U” in the
HKSAR smart ID
.
Dependent visa/ entry permit
Visa under the
Immigration Arrangements for Non-local Graduates (IANG)
Work permit (for part-time students only)

FAQs
or check with

enquiry@immd.gov.hk
].

Upon receipt of a properly filed application, the University will act as your sponsor, monitor the application progress and update the online visa application status.
After IMMD approval, the University will advise you to download the e-Visa for entry to Hong Kong for study.

Visa application takes time. You are advised to arrive at HKUST two weeks before the program begins and you are allowed to register as a student only if you have obtained the student visa/ entry permit. The earlier you apply, the higher confidence level that you will receive the visa label in time.

You will not be able to register as a student if you fail to obtain the student visa/ entry permit in time.

title: Accommodation | Office of Student Affairs
text:
requently Asked Questions
Accommodation
On-campus Student Hostels
The University provides 2,600 on-campus
student hostel
places for our students. Eligible local and non-local undergraduate and postgraduate students can apply for student hostel rooms. Hostel rooms will be allocated according to the Hostel Residency Policy (HRP) and the allocation priorities. In 2024-25, the University has approved to set aside certain number of hostel places and quotas for full-time
self-financed
UG and PG students and they should contact their programme offices for hostel application and allocation details. The campus and student hostels of Lingnan University, like many places in Hong Kong, smoking is strictly prohibited in
all
areas of the campus.
Hostel Room and Facilities

title: Accommodation | Office of Student Affairs
text:
ty, like many places in Hong Kong, smoking is strictly prohibited in
all
areas of the campus.
Hostel Room and Facilities
All student rooms are for double occupancy or triple occupancy. The size of a student room is 110 to 130 sq ft. Each student resident will have one bed, one cabinet, one desk and one chair. Free LAN, WiFi service and electricity are provided. The air-conditioning fee will be shared by roommates in the same room.
Shared washrooms by residents of two rooms will be provided in Jockey Club New Hall, Wong Hoo Chuen Hall and Wu Jieh Yee Hall. The other 6 hostels provide communal washrooms (shared by the residents of the whole floor). Refrigerators, boilers and microwave ovens are installed in the common pantry of each floor.

title: Accommodation | Office of Student Affairs
text:
idents of the whole floor). Refrigerators, boilers and microwave ovens are installed in the common pantry of each floor.
The Laundry room of each hostel is equipped with washing machines (HK$6 per cycle) and dryers (HK$1 per 7 minutes), which are all Octopus Card-operated. For more details on hostel facilities, please click
https://www.ln.edu.hk/osa/residential-life/student-hostel/hostel-facilities
.
Hostel Rules and Regulations
Each hostel has a Warden, a Senior Tutor and a group of Tutors. As a hostel resident, student needs to comply with hostel rules and regulations, which are listed at
https://www.ln.edu.hk/osa/residential-life/student-hostel/hostel-rules-and-residency-policy.
Hostel/Room Assignment

title: Accommodation | Office of Student Affairs
text:
d at
https://www.ln.edu.hk/osa/residential-life/student-hostel/hostel-rules-and-residency-policy.
Hostel/Room Assignment
To facilitate cross-cultural appreciation and integration in the Lingnan residential community, students with different cultural backgrounds will be paired up as roommates based on their personal preferences. There will be different application exercises for undergraduate, exchange students, and research/ taught postgraduate students.
Most beds are either 1.98/ 2.13 meters in length. If you are taller than 1.85 meters, please let us know in advance so that we can try to arrange a room with a bed of 2.13 meters in length.
Phone Calls and Internet Connections at Student Hostels

title: Accommodation | Office of Student Affairs
text:
e can try to arrange a room with a bed of 2.13 meters in length.
Phone Calls and Internet Connections at Student Hostels
Free public telephones for local calls are available at the common lounge on each student bedroom floor and the common room floor. Free LAN and WiFi service are available in the hostel room, but it is safer for students to bring their own LAN cable for connecting their own PC to the Data Outlet socket in the room. Students can find the set-up guides for internet access for different computers and systems at the
ITSC website
or contact the ITSC hotline at 2616 7995 after arrival.

FAQs
What are the programmes of School of Graduate Studies (GS) and When is the application period for 2026 entry?
ALL applicants are reminded to read the GS’ website carefully before submitting an application.
Applications for entry in 2026/27 begin on 01 Oct 2025. The deadlines for applications vary by programme. Please refer to the above website for the deadlines specific to individual programmes.
How can I submit my application?
You can apply for our taught postgraduate programmes via the online application system.

title: FAQs | Taught Postgraduate Programmes
text:
How can I submit my application?
You can apply for our taught postgraduate programmes via the online application system.
Can I apply for more than one programme? Will it affect my chance of success?
You are required to submit separate applications if you wish to make multiple applications for different programmes of our School. Please also note that multiple application fees should be paid. Your chance of success will not be affected.
However, applicants are not encouraged to switch to another programme once an admission offer has been accepted. Please be aware that the deposit fee will not be refunded if you decide to switch to another programme.
Can I apply for admission if i am still in my last year of study?

title: FAQs | Taught Postgraduate Programmes
text:
refunded if you decide to switch to another programme.
Can I apply for admission if i am still in my last year of study?
You do not need to graduate from your bachelor’s degree (for master’s degree applications) or master’s degree (for doctoral applications) at the time of application. However, you must obtain the respective degree on or before August 15 of your admission year.You do not need to graduate from your bachelor’s degree (for master’s degree applications) or master’s degree (for doctoral applications) at the time of application. However, you must obtain the respective degree on or before August 15 of your admission year.
What kind of supporting documents will be needed for considering my application?
Please click the website for details of the required supporting documents.

title: FAQs | Taught Postgraduate Programmes
text:
ill be needed for considering my application?
Please click the website for details of the required supporting documents.
Do you consider IELTS One Skill Retake score for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement for master's programmes?
For master's programmes, IELTS One Skill Retake score is not accepted for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement. The University only considers the IELTS result from an individual test on one single test date.
If my TOEFL or IELTS score does not meet the requirement of the programme, could I use other means to demonstrate my English proficiency? Can the CET conducted by universities in China be used to substitute TOEFL or IELTS?

title: FAQs | Taught Postgraduate Programmes
text:
demonstrate my English proficiency? Can the CET conducted by universities in China be used to substitute TOEFL or IELTS?
Generally, applicants are required to submit TOEFL or IELTS scores that meet the programme's requirements as proof of English proficiency. If you have a CET6 score, you may also submit it for the Admissions Committee's consideration. However, acceptance is ultimately subject to the specific policies of each programme.
How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted the application?
Upon successful payment of the application fee and submission of an application, an email notification with application no. will be sent to the applicant.
Do I need to attend an interview?

title: FAQs | Taught Postgraduate Programmes
text:
application, an email notification with application no. will be sent to the applicant.
Do I need to attend an interview?
Shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview by the Programme Office within 2 to 3 weeks. An applicant who has not been invited to an interview shall not assume that he/she is not being considered for admission.
What language will the interview be conducted in?
The interview will typically be conducted in English.
Do I need to submit reference letters?
You can invite your referees to complete the recommendation letters (sample could be downloaded from the online admission system) and to send us by email admission office if they are not convenient to post it.

title: FAQs | Taught Postgraduate Programmes
text:
loaded from the online admission system) and to send us by email admission office if they are not convenient to post it.
Regarding the reference letter, kindly be alerted that we will not accept reference letters from personal email address(e.g., yahoo, Hotmail, gmail or other similar public system). Please inform your referees to send the soft copy of recommendation letters by their official, company or professional email address.

title: Information for Non-local Applicants | Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office (TPO)
text:
Applications
Application Procedure
Leaflets and Forms
Information for Non-local Applicants
Get Prepared to Study
in a New Environment
Studying in a new place you have never been to can be challenging. Don't worry. Read on and you will learn the essential information on studying in Hong Kong as a non-local and what to expect after graduation.
Student Visa
Non-local students studying in Hong Kong are required to apply for student visas. Details of the entry arrangement are set out in the
Immigration Department's website
.
Visa Application Form
Should your application for our master's degree programme be successful, an offer pack containing a visa application form and instructions will be sent to you. The visa application form can also be downloaded from the
Immigration Department's website

title: Information for Non-local Applicants | Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office (TPO)
text:
ructions will be sent to you. The visa application form can also be downloaded from the
Immigration Department's website
. The completed visa application form together with supporting documents should be returned to us for our handling.
Visa Label
Once your visa application is approved, we will collect the visa label and send it to you by courier.
Visa Application Fee
A visa application fee of HKD700 (for 2026/27) will be charged for each visa application, which covers the fee payable to the Immigration Department. The visa application fee should be paid together with the first instalment of the tuition fee and is non-refundable and non-transferable regardless of the visa application result, which is at the discretion of the Immigration Department.
The Immigration Department's website
Accommodation
Off-campus Residence

title: Information for Non-local Applicants | Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office (TPO)
text:
at the discretion of the Immigration Department.
The Immigration Department's website
Accommodation
Off-campus Residence
Private rental accommodation is available near the University. Monthly rental will depend on the size, location and type of accommodation. Non-local students may consider sharing a flat with a few roommates. Information on private rental accommodation will be provided by Programme Teams.
Staying in Hong Kong after Graduation
Graduates of full-time TPg programmes can apply to stay in Hong Kong for 24 months after graduation under the Immigration Arrangements for Non-local Graduates (IANG), subject to meeting of the eligibility criteria. Details are available in the
Immigration Department's website
.
Part-time Jobs

title: Information for Non-local Applicants | Taught Postgraduate Programmes Office (TPO)
text:
t to meeting of the eligibility criteria. Details are available in the
Immigration Department's website
.
Part-time Jobs
Starting from November 1 2023, full-time non-local postgraduate students would be temporarily exempted from the restrictions on taking up part-time jobs. The arrangement will be implemented on a trial basis for two years. For details, please refer to the
Immigration Department's website
.
Disclaimer:
The information provided on this page is for your reference only. Please refer to the relevant official websites for the most accurate information and latest updates.

title: Residential Life | Student Affairs Office
text:
University-managed Accommodations
The Student Halls of Residence in Hung Hom and Homantin are home to over 4600 students from diverse cultures, backgrounds and disciplines. Frequently Asked Questions
General Information
1. What are the differences between Taught Postgraduate programmes and Research programmes?
Taught Postgraduate programmes focus more on coursework. Some may require the completion of a dissertation. For research programmes, the PhD or MPhil degree is awarded to a student who, upon completing an approved programme of study and research, presents a dissertation on the results of his/her research and satisfies the examiners in an oral examination (and other examinations if required) in matters relevant to the subject of the dissertation. For details about our Research Postgraduate programmes, please click
here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/research-postgraduate)
.

2. When is the application period for admission to Taught Postgraduate programmes?
The September 2027 Entry will start inviting applications from 2 July 2026, and s
ome programmes may invite applications for January entry. Please click
here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate)
for the most up-to-date information.

Once you have selected a suitable programme, you are encouraged to apply as early as possible since our academic departments will consider applications on a rolling basis until all the places are filled.
4. How do I apply?
Applications are received online via
eAdmission system (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do)

Please click

for the definition of non-local applicants.
The levels of tuition fees for local and non-local students are different for some programmes. It is therefore important for applicants to indicate their identity correctly when they submit their application(s).
6. What are the entrance requirements for admission to Taught Postgraduate programmes?
Please click

7. What are the selection criteria for admission?
Applications will be considered on the basis of the information (such as academic achievements, professional qualifications and work experience) provided in the online application form.
8. Can I apply for admission if I do not meet the stipulated entrance requirements?
Applicants with good calibre but without the relevant qualifications may also be admitted. These applicants must demonstrate sufficient motivation, knowledge and potential to complete the programme successfully.
9. Does PolyU accept UKVI IELTS for applying to taught postgraduate programmes?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
to complete the programme successfully.
9. Does PolyU accept UKVI IELTS for applying to taught postgraduate programmes?
The contents and format for both IELTS and IELTS UKVI tests are actually the same. The UKVI number is for reference of the UK Government only. Therefore, you can continue using your IELTS UKVI result to apply for admission to our taught postgraduate programmes.
10. Can I apply for more than one programme at the same time?
Multiple applications are allowed for applicants seeking admission to our taught postgraduate programmes. You can make another application under the same account after you have submitted one application.
11. Can I apply for credit transfer?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:

11. Can I apply for credit transfer?
Applicants who have previously studied at a university, polytechnic or other post-secondary institution and completed subjects that are relevant to their chosen programme may be eligible for credit transfer. Please click
here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/credit-transfer-and-exemption-tpg)
for details of the policy governing credit transfer.
12. What can I do if I do not intend to pursue a formal academic award but am interested in some subjects offered under some programmes?
The University has a provision for applicants to enrol on individual subjects which is processed on a semester basis. Please click
here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/subject-based-admission-tpg)
for details.

13. What is the meaning of "Mixed-mode" study?
Classes are held in both daytime and evening. Students may attend classes in the daytime, evening or a combination of both. Mixed-mode programmes give students an option to engage in a full-time or part-time study load. This provides flexibility for them to determine their own pace of study.
14. Am I eligible for the financial assistance scheme?
Please click
here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/financial-assistance-and-scholarships-tpg)
for details.

15. Are non-local students eligible for financial assistance? Can they take up part-time jobs?
Non-local students are not eligible for financial assistance from both the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region and PolyU. Therefore students have to ensure that they have adequate funds to cover tuition fees, accommodation and other expenses, including medical insurance before accepting the offer to study at PolyU.
Notwithstanding the above, non-local students studying a full-time programme at degree level or above with a study period of not less than one academic year will be allowed to take up part-time on-campus employment and summer jobs subject to the conditions as stipulated by the Immigration Department of Hong Kong Special Administrative Region. Please refer to the

Student Handbook (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/ar/web/en/for-polyu-students/student-handbook/index.html)
for details.
16. Are non-local students eligible for applying for hall residence?
Students of Taught Postgraduate programmes are NOT eligible for Students Halls of Residence. They have to secure their own accommodation in Hong Kong and may approach the Non-local Student Services Team (NLSST) for one-stop advisory service. Please click
here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/sao/student-resources-and-support-section/non-local-student-services/)

Before Application Submission
17. How much is the application fee?
The application fee for admission to taught postgraduate programmes for 2027/28 is HK$600, except for Master of Business Administration (MBA) and doctorate programmes of Faculty of Business. The application fee for MBA is $800, while the fee of doctorate programmes of Faculty of Business is $1,200 for 2027/28 intake cohort. The fee is non-refundable and non-transferrable. You must settle the application fee upon submission of your online application, otherwise, your application will not be further processed. Please note that other than the specified application fee, you are not required to pay any money to any persons in connection with your application.
18. What should I do if my results are not yet available when I submit my application?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
connection with your application.
18. What should I do if my results are not yet available when I submit my application?
If you have not yet received your results/transcript(s)/certificate(s) when you apply, please indicate in your application the date on which they will be available. You should update your results and upload your final official transcript(s)/certificate(s) to your application on or before the date which you have specified.
19. Should I submit additional/supporting documents?
You are required to upload your identity document transcript(s)/certificate(s) in support of your qualifications and other documents (including official English translation if your documents are not in English) as specifically required for your programme choices when you submit your application. Please check the details at our
Study@PolyU (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate)

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
it your application. Please check the details at our
Study@PolyU (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate)
website (select “Applying to PolyU” at the top menu bar > click the “Additional Documents Required” section).
Failure to complete the online application form properly and failure to submit the required documents may affect your chances of admission.
You need not submit more documents than required as non-required documents will not be considered by departments. The academic department concerned may, in the selection process, approach you for clarification of your qualifications.
20. Should I submit documents to programme host departments directly?
No. Please submit them together with your online application form, unless specified by individual programme host department.
21. What should I do if my academic documents are not in English?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
ess specified by individual programme host department.
21. What should I do if my academic documents are not in English?
Please provide officially translated copies of the documents in addition to copies of the original documents; otherwise your application may not be considered.
22. Is IELTS or TOEFL compulsory for admission?
Generally students are expected to fulfil
English Language Requirement (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/admission-requirements-tpg)
, English language qualifications other than IELTS and TOEFL will be considered individually on their own merits by the departments concerned. Applicants may be required to attend interviews or tests to further demonstrate their language proficiency.
23. What should I do if I want to apply for credit transfer?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
or tests to further demonstrate their language proficiency.
23. What should I do if I want to apply for credit transfer?
You are not required to specify this when you submit your application. The departments concerned will decide if credit transfer can be granted based on your qualifications. The total credits you are required to study will be shown on your Notice of Offer if you are given a study place.
24. Can I change my programme choice after submission?
You are not allowed to change your programme choice after your application has been submitted. However, you may submit another application under the same account after you have submitted one application.
25. Can I apply for re-admission?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
t another application under the same account after you have submitted one application.
25. Can I apply for re-admission?
If you have withdrawn from a programme of study at PolyU without completing the withdrawal procedures or have been required to withdraw in the previous academic year, you will not be considered for re-admission to the same programme in this academic year. Any fees paid will not be refunded. You are however still eligible to apply for admission to other programmes.
26. How will PolyU use the information collected from my application?
All information we collect from your application form will only be used for processing your application for admission to our programmes and for statistical and research purposes. Please refer to the
Personal Information Collection Statement (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/ar/pics/)
of PolyU for details.

of PolyU for details.
After Application Submission
27. How can I settle my application fee?
You will see a payment reminder page after you have submitted your application. You can go directly to the "Payment Page" to settle your application fee immediately. Alternatively, you can log into your eAdmission account before the payment deadline to settle the fee at a later stage. For details of the payment methods, please click
here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/fo/students/fee-and-payment/payment-methods-for-applicants/)
.

.
It normally takes 2 working days to transfer the payment record to PolyU, but it takes 5 working days if you pay via Flywire. Please make sure that your application fee is properly settled before the specified payment deadline. Otherwise, your application will not be further processed. We will accordingly cancel your application when the payment status is "Overdue". (Please log into your eAdmission account to check whether the payment status has been updated to "Paid" after you have settled the fee.)
28. Is the application fee refundable?
The application fee is non-refundable and non-transferrable.
29. Where can I find my application number?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
fee refundable?
The application fee is non-refundable and non-transferrable.
29. Where can I find my application number?
An application number will be assigned to you and shown on your online application once you have successfully submitted your application.
30. What should I do if I want to submit additional/supporting documents after application submission?
You are allowed to submit further documents after application submission, except if you are being considered for an offer. Please scan the documents and log into your eAdmission account for the upload.
You may wish to check the supporting documents specifically required by individual programmes at our
Study@PolyU (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate)

pecifically required by individual programmes at our

website (select “Applying to PolyU” at the top menu bar > click the “Additional Documents Required” section). Please, however, note that you need not submit more documents than required as non-required documents will not be considered by departments.
31. How can I verify my application data?
Please log into your eAdmission account to verify your application data. If you cannot find your application data, please
contact us (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/enquiry)
immediately (please quote your application number in all your correspondences with PolyU).

As the information you entered in the online application form will form the basis on which your application will be considered, you should make sure that it is complete and accurate. Otherwise, your chances of admission may be affected, and any offer made will also be withdrawn if it is based on incomplete and inaccurate application data.
32. How can I update my application data?
Most of the data which you have entered, except for the programme choice(s), can be changed after your application has been submitted but before you are being considered for an offer. If you have indicated that your results are not yet available by filling out the "Update Result Date", you can update your results on or before the date specified.

Otherwise, your chances of admission may be affected, and any offer made will also be withdrawn if it is based on incomplete and inaccurate application data.
33. How can I know my application result?
If you are given an offer or a conditional offer, you will be informed via email/SMS. You may also log into your eAdmission account from time to time to check the progress of your application.

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
ia email/SMS. You may also log into your eAdmission account from time to time to check the progress of your application.
If there is no news by early September (for September entry) or mid-January (for January entry), you can assume that your application is unsuccessful. No separate notification will be sent for unsuccessful cases.
Upon Receiving Offer
34. What should I do to accept an offer or a conditional offer?
Please indicate your acceptance and settle the registration fee or tuition fee before the "last day for payment" displayed in the Notice of Offer, and register on the programme registration date as indicated on the Notice of Offer. (For conditional offer, you need to complete the procedures for meeting the conditions stated in the Notice of Offer beforehand.)
35. How can I complete the meet condition(s) procedure?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
eeting the conditions stated in the Notice of Offer beforehand.)
35. How can I complete the meet condition(s) procedure?
For meeting condition(s), please return to the Online Notice of Offer page via eAdmission to complete the procedures for "Meeting Conditions" when you have acquired the relevant qualifications on or before the "Meet Condition Date".
36. What should I do to accept another offer from PolyU after I have paid the tuition fees for an earlier offer?
Please
contact us (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/enquiry)
to transfer the fees already paid for the earlier offer to the other offer. Please remember to provide application number of both offers in your request. This arrangement only applies if both programmes lead to PolyU awards.
37. Can I enrol on two programmes at the same time?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
is arrangement only applies if both programmes lead to PolyU awards.
37. Can I enrol on two programmes at the same time?
Students are not allowed to enrol concurrently on two full-time/sandwich programmes whether or not one of the programmes is offered by another institution. Except for programmes which do not lead to any formal award, students are not allowed to enrol concurrently on a full-time/sandwich programme and a part-time programme, or more than one part-time programmes, including those offered by another institution without any approval from the Head of your academic Department.
38. Is the tuition fee paid refundable?
All fees paid will not be refunded except in the case of programme cancellation.
39. When will the offer of a programme marked with "subject to approval" be confirmed?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
e case of programme cancellation.
39. When will the offer of a programme marked with "subject to approval" be confirmed?
You can assume that the offer is confirmed unless you are separately notified of its non-approval.
40. What if the programme I have applied for is cancelled?
In that case, both the tuition fee and application fee paid will be refunded to you.
41. When should I submit my student visa application?
It will normally take 8-10 weeks for the Immigration Department of HKSAR to process your application, therefore, you should complete and return the student visa application form with all the required documents to us by express post as soon as possible after you have accepted our offer. For details, please refer to offer confirmation section of the Notice of Offer in eAdmission.
42. Do I need to provide proof of financial means with my student visa application?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
f the Notice of Offer in eAdmission.
42. Do I need to provide proof of financial means with my student visa application?
The Hong Kong Immigration Department requires all non-local students to provide proof of their ability to pay tuition fees and living expenses upon submission of their student visa applications. This proof can take the form of personal or family bank letters/bank statements.
43. How can I check the status of my student visa application?
You can check the latest status of your student visa application via
eAdmission (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do)
from time to time.
44. When can I receive my e-Visa?

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)
text:
ion via
eAdmission (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do)
from time to time.
44. When can I receive my e-Visa?
Once your visa application is approved, you will receive an email notification on how to download your e-Visa from the Hong Kong Immigration Department’s website. However, if you are given a conditional offer, you will only receive the email notification after you have met the condition(s) of your admission offer.
45. How should I submit my original test score report (i.e., GMAT, GRE, IETLS or TOEFL)?
If your score reports can be verified online, you should inform the relevant test/ examination provider to release the right to PolyU to verify your results online. PolyU's institutional code is CB8 for GMAT, 3404 for GRE and 0732 for TOEFL.

title: Frequently Asked Questions (TPG) | The Hong Kong Polytechnic University (PolyU)

ght to PolyU to verify your results online. PolyU's institutional code is CB8 for GMAT, 3404 for GRE and 0732 for TOEFL.
IETLS score report could be verified online without an access code. You are therefore not required to request IELTS to send us the hard copy report unless you are asked to do so.
46. How can I check the status of qualification verification?
You can check the latest status of qualification verification via
eAdmission (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do)
from time to time.
47. How to submit required documents for qualification verification?
Please refer to the Guidelines on Submission of Required Documents for Qualification Verification (
link (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/aradm/tpg/guidelines_qualification_verification.pdf)

nts for Qualification Verification (

) for details. Please note that all qualifications (except those obtained from PolyU and CityU) mentioned in your application should be submitted for verifications. Failure to do so or any discrepancies found may lead to nullification of your offer and registration at the University. Fees paid will not be refunded. You can only proceed with online programme registration after completion of qualification verification and approval of student visa application (if applicable).

title: Orientation and Welcoming Weeks | Student Affairs Office
text:
To enjoy the exciting start of your journey at PolyU, Non-Local Student Services Team (NLSST) of Student Affairs Office has organized various welcome orientation activities at the beginning of semester in order to assist non-local freshmen to transit to PolyU-life.
Students could gain necessary information and assistance from the team and make connections with other students through the events.

title: Registration / Enrolment Arrangements
text:
Programme Registration for New Admittees
You will receive an e-mail/SMS on the offer. You can then access to your application and check the online Notice of Offer.
You will need to settle the tuition fee before the payment deadline.
Please refer to the "Registration Procedures" stated on your "Online Notice of Offer" for programme registration and get your student ID card at the designated venue.
The study place will be offered to other applicants if you fail to settle the tuition fee in full and/or complete registration on time.
Enrolment for Current Students
You will receive an email notification for enrolment from the Finance Office (FO). You can log on the FO System at
https://www40.polyu.edu.hk/fosae/
to view the payment details.
You will need to settle the tuition fee before the payment deadline.
Please contact FO at
fostud@polyu.edu.hk

title: When is the application period for 2026 entry and when is the application deadline?
text:
Question: When is the application period for 2026 entry and when is the application deadline?
Answer: Applications for entry in 2026/27 begin on 16 September 2025. The deadlines for applications vary by programme. Please refer to the online programme information for the deadlines specific to individual programmes. Applications for some programmes are considered on a rolling basis. Review of applications will start before the deadline and continue until all places are filled. Early applications are therefore strongly encouraged. Late applications may be accepted at the discretion of the respective academic unit.

title: Where can I obtain the application information?
text:
Question: Where can I obtain the application information?
Answer: For application information about master's programmes, research degree and professional doctorate degree programmes, please visit the website of the Chow Yei Ching School of Graduate Studies.

title: What is the provisional academic calendar for the 2026/27 academic year?
text:
Question: What is the provisional academic calendar for the 2026/27 academic year?
Answer: Our academic year consists of two 16-week semesters (i.e., Semesters A and B) and an optional 9-week summer term. For the 2026/27 academic year, the provisional calendar is: Semester A: 31 August 2026 to 19 December 2026 Semester B: 11 January 2027 to 10 May 2027 Summer Term: 7 June 2027 to 7 August 2027

title: What is the timetable for morning and evening classes?
text:
Question: What is the timetable for morning and evening classes?
Answer: The timetable is set as follows: Monday to Friday 9:00 am – 9:50 pm (Evening classes normally start at 7:00 pm. Classes with a duration of more than 3 hours may start earlier at 6:30 pm or extend to 10:50 pm if applicable.) Saturday 9:00 am – 6:50 pm For the timetable of individual course(s), please consult the respective academic unit.

title: What is the medium of instruction of the University?
text:
Question: What is the medium of instruction of the University?
Answer: The medium of instruction and assessment of the University is English, unless otherwise specified for a particular course or programme.

title: Are there any scholarships available for my study at CityUHK?
text:
Question: Are there any scholarships available for my study at CityUHK?
Answer: For details of scholarship opportunities, please contact the programme offering academic unit. Contact details are available here.

title: If I have a physical or other disability, do I need to declare it at the application stage?
text:
Question: If I have a physical or other disability, do I need to declare it at the application stage?
Answer: Admission to the University is based on academic criteria and other specified entrance requirements. If you have a physical or other disability, you are not required to declare this at the application stage. However, if you need assistance regarding your application (e.g., to attend an interview), you may discuss your needs with the programme offering academic unit so that special arrangements can be made if practicable.

title: What will happen if the programme I have applied for is subsequently cancelled?
text:
Question: What will happen if the programme I have applied for is subsequently cancelled?
Answer: The University reserves the right to cancel any programmes, in which case all fees paid by applicants will be refunded.

title: How should I send my IELTS/TOEFL results to CityUHK?
text:
Question: How should I send my IELTS/TOEFL results to CityUHK?
Answer: You should arrange for your IELTS/TOEFL results to be sent to the University as soon as possible after you have submitted your application. If you have applied for more than one programme, the IELTS/TOEFL report will be shared among all your submitted applications. Submission of IELTS result to CityUHK Please arrange for it to be sent via the IELTS Results Service e-delivery to ‘City University of Hong Kong - Graduate School. Submission of TOEFL result to CityUHK Please arrange with the Education Testing Service (ETS) to send the score reportit directly to the University. The TOEFL institution code for CityUHK is 3401. TOEFL iBT Home Edition, TOEFL MyBest Score, IELTS Indicator, IELTS One Skill Retake, and IELTS Online are not accepted.

title: What are the address and opening hours of the Chow Yei Ching School of Graduate Studies?
