title: I am now studying the first year of a master’s degree overseas and I wish to transfer my study to CityUHK. Can the credits that I earned be transferred to the CityUHK programme?
text:
Question: I am now studying the first year of a master’s degree overseas and I wish to transfer my study to CityUHK. Can the credits that I earned be transferred to the CityUHK programme?
Answer: If you have studied similar courses at a recognised post-secondary institution, you may be eligible for credit transfer to meet some of the programme requirements. Applications for credit transfer for coursework completed prior to entry to the University must be made in the first semester following admission, before the deadline set by the University. Transferred credit units must have been earned no earlier than eight years before enrolment at the University. Individual academic units may stipulate a more stringent requirement for transferred credit units, as deemed appropriate. Please contact the programme offering academic unit for more details. Transferred credits will not be counted in the calculation of the Grade Point Average (GPA) and classification of the award. Credit transfer should be limited to a maximum of 30% of the total credit units for the award being pursued. B - Academic Programmes & Study Mode

title: Which programmes invite applications?
text:
Question: Which programmes invite applications?
Answer: Information about the programmes that are open for applications is available at the online programme list. If you have questions concerning the curriculum and other programme information, please contact the relevant academic unit directly.

title: What are the differences between master's programmes and research degree programmes?
text:
Question: What are the differences between master's programmes and research degree programmes?
Answer: Master's programmes focus more on coursework, some programmes may require the completion of a project or dissertation. MPhil and PhD research degrees consist of both coursework and independent research studies culminating in the submission of a thesis. For details, please refer to the website of the Chow Yei Ching School of Graduate Studies. B03. Can I apply for programmes offered in 'combined mode'? (applicable to non-local applicants) Non-local applicants will be admitted to these programmes for either full-time or part-time studies. Non-local students must maintain the required credit load for their full-time or part-time studies and any changes will require approval from the University. C - Status of Applicants

title: Am I a local or a non-local applicant?
text:
Question: Am I a local or a non-local applicant?
Answer: The definitions of non-local applicants and local applicants are set out below (with effect from Semester A 2019/20 entry): Non-local Applicants Persons holding the following documents issued by Immigration Department (IMMD) of the HKSAR are classified as non-local applicants: Student visa / entry permit; or Visa / entry permit under the Immigration Arrangements for Non-local Graduates (IANG Visa); or Dependant visa / entry permit for applicants who were 18 years old or above when they were issued with such visa / entry permit by IMMD For information about student visa/entry permit, please visit the official website of IMMD of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR). Local Applicants Persons holding any of the following documents issued by Immigration Department (IMMD) of the HKSAR are classified as local applicants: Hong Kong Identity Card (Permanent) Documents issued by IMMD showing right to land / right of abode in Hong Kong One-way permit for entry to Hong Kong Full-time employment visa / work permit (for part-time study) Dependant visa / entry permit for applicants who were below 18 years old when they were first issued with such visa / entry permit by IMMD

title: What should I do if I have wrongly identified myself in my application as a local or non-local applicant?
text:
Question: What should I do if I have wrongly identified myself in my application as a local or non-local applicant?
Answer: If you have wrongly identified yourself as a local or non-local applicant, you should notify the Chow Yei Ching School of Graduate Studies in writing as soon as possible. Please provide your name, application number, a copy of your Hong Kong Identity Card and a copy of your student visa / entry permit (if any). It will take 7 working days to update your status in our application system. You may login your application account for checking. The personal information collected by the University will be used for the purpose of record verification and processing of your application. Such information may be transferred to relevant parties within the University or to government departments for various types of processing related to your application. Your personal data will be retained only for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected and in accordance with the University’s record retention policies. For details, please refer to the Privacy Policy Statement and Personal Information Collection Statement related to your admission application. D - Entrance Requirements

title: What are the entrance requirements for admission to the University? How do I know whether I meet the entrance requirements for a particular programme?
text:
Question: What are the entrance requirements for admission to the University? How do I know whether I meet the entrance requirements for a particular programme?
Answer: For admission to the programmes offered by the University, applicants must meet the General Entrance Requirements AND Programme Entrance Requirements AND / OR English Proficiency Requirements as specified for individual programmes, if applicable.

title: If I have fulfilled both the General Entrance Requirement and Programme Entrance Requirement, does it mean that I am likely to be admitted?
text:
Question: If I have fulfilled both the General Entrance Requirement and Programme Entrance Requirement, does it mean that I am likely to be admitted?
Answer: The General Entrance Requirements and Programme Entrance Requirements of a programme stipulate the minimum requirements that applicants are expected to fulfil. Fulfilment of these requirements does not guarantee admission to the programme. The assessment for admission is based on a number of factors, including the applicant’s academic and professional qualifications, work experience, performance in the interview or test and other criteria as required by the respective programmes.

title: Will I be invited to attend an admission interview / admission test?
text:
Question: Will I be invited to attend an admission interview / admission test?
Answer: Not all programmes require an interview or test and the arrangements also vary from year to year. Please consult individual academic unit for details of the interview arrangement, if any.

title: What is the English language requirement for admission to CityUHK programmes?
text:
Question: What is the English language requirement for admission to CityUHK programmes?
Answer: For master's programmes, applicants whose entrance qualification is obtained from an institution where the medium of instruction is not English should also fulfil one of the following minimum English proficiency requirements^: a score of 79 (internet-based test) in the Test of English as a Foreign Language (TOEFL)@#; or an overall band score of 6.0 in International English Language Testing System (IELTS)@##; or band 6 in the Chinese Mainland’s College English Test (CET-6)*; or other equivalent qualifications. ^Individual Colleges and Schools may stipulate a higher English proficiency requirement, or equivalent acceptable qualifications, as deemed appropriate for their disciplines. @ TOEFL and IELTS scores are considered valid for two years. Applicants are required to provide their English test results obtained within the two years preceding the start of the University's application period. 'TOEFL iBT Home Edition', ‘TOEFL MyBest Score’, ‘IELTS Indicator’, ‘IELTS One Skill Retake’ and ‘IELTS Online’ are not acceptable. # Applicants are required to arrange with the Educational Testing Service (ETS) to send their TOEFL results directly to the University. The TOEFL institution code for CityUHK is 3401. ## Applicants are required to arrange for sending their IELTS result(s) via the IELTS Results Service e-delivery to the University. Please note that applicants with an overall band score of 6.0 will be required to pass an interview for English proficiency conducted by the concerned academic unit. Interviews for other applicants may not be required.

title: When should I arrange to send my IELTS/TOEFL score? If I have previously arranged to send my score to CityU, should I arrange to send it again?
text:
Question: When should I arrange to send my IELTS/TOEFL score? If I have previously arranged to send my score to CityU, should I arrange to send it again?
Answer: You should arrange for your IELTS/TOEFL results to be sent to the University as soon as possible after you have submitted your application. If you have applied for more than one programme, the uploaded IELTS/TOEFL results will be shared among all your submitted applications.

title: Do you consider TOEFL MyBest Scores (iBT) / IELTS One Skill Retake score for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement for master's programmes?
text:
Question: Do you consider TOEFL MyBest Scores (iBT) / IELTS One Skill Retake score for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement for master's programmes?
Answer: For master's programmes, 'TOEFL MyBest Scores' / IELTS One Skill Retake score is not accepted for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement. The University only considers the TOEFL Scores (iBT) / IELTS result from an individual test on one single test date. Individual Colleges and Schools may stipulate a higher English proficiency requirement, or equivalent acceptable qualifications, as deemed appropriate for their disciplines. Please refer to our website for more information on the entrance requirements.

title: The TOEFL iBT has introduced a new 1-6 score scale, and my score report shows both scores [e.g. new score scale:4 & old score scale:79] under the total score. Is it acceptable?
text:
Question: The TOEFL iBT has introduced a new 1-6 score scale, and my score report shows both scores [e.g. new score scale:4 & old score scale:79] under the total score. Is it acceptable?
Answer: Please note that starting from 21 January 2026, the TOEFL iBT introduces a new 1-6 score scale. During a two-year transition period, a comparable overall score (0-120) will be included on the TOEFL iBT score reports. The new score reports, issued after that date, are acceptable as they continue to display the overall score on the 0-120 scale. Applicants can refer to the information here on the new 1-6 score scale introduced by the TOEFL iBT or here on the English Proficiency Requirements of the University.

title: I am a current CityUHK student on a master's programme. Can I apply to study for an additional programme or change my study programme at CityUHK?
text:
Question: I am a current CityUHK student on a master's programme. Can I apply to study for an additional programme or change my study programme at CityUHK?
Answer: As concurrent enrolment in two CityUHK programmes is normally not allowed, you will need to withdraw from your current programme before enrolling in a new programme. Current CityUHK students who wish to change their study programme to another one of the same level should apply for programme transfer. E - Fees / Payment

title: What are the tuition fees for the 2026/27 academic year?
text:
Question: What are the tuition fees for the 2026/27 academic year?
Answer: For details of the tuition fees for individual programmes, please refer here for information.

title: How much is the online application fee? How can I pay the fee?
text:
Question: How much is the online application fee? How can I pay the fee?
Answer: The online application fee per programme choice is HK$600 except for EMBA programmes (P01A, P01B & P12) which is HK$1,000. Payment can be made by using VISA, MasterCard, UnionPay*, Alipay HK/ China, Apple Pay, WeChat Pay, Faster Payment System (FPS) or PayMe. If you wish to apply for more than one programme, you may submit an additional application. Please note that if you receive more than one admission offer from the University, you can choose to enrol in one programme only. The application fee is non-refundable and non-transferable. Your application will only be processed after the application fee is paid. *UnionPay service does not support Microsoft EDGE browser. Please use Microsoft IE or Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox.

title: What can I do if I have problem in getting through the online payment gateway for settling the application/acceptance fee?
text:
Question: What can I do if I have problem in getting through the online payment gateway for settling the application/acceptance fee?
Answer: If you encounter problems with the online payment gateway, please take the following actions: Make sure the web browser you are using is either Microsoft IE version 11, Google Chrome, or Mozilla Firefox with TLSv1.2 enabled (for IE: Internet Options > Advanced > use TLSv1.2). Ensure the web browser is configured to enable Javascript and turn on cookie especially for users using Apple iOS (e.g. MacBook / iPhone) where the system disables the cookie as default. If your browser is set to block pop-up windows, please set it to allow pop-ups temporarily. Make sure your firewall setting allows access to the CityUHK domain site. UnionPay service does not support Microsoft EDGE browser. Please use Microsoft IE or Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox.

title: I failed to pay the application fee online. Can I make the payment by other means?
text:
Question: I failed to pay the application fee online. Can I make the payment by other means?
Answer: For online applications, you can pay the application fee by using VISA, MasterCard, UnionPay*, Alipay HK/ China, Apple Pay, WeChat Pay, Faster Payment System (FPS) or PayMe. Please note that sometimes payment may fail due to a system error. You are advised to save your data and make the payment again later. Your application will only be processed after the application fee is paid. *UnionPay service does not support Microsoft EDGE browser. Please use Microsoft IE or Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox.

title: What should I do if I made a duplicate payment?
text:
Question: What should I do if I made a duplicate payment?
Answer: If a duplicate payment is made, please notify the Chow Yei Ching School of Graduate Studies in writing to apply for a refund. We will look into your case and arrange for a refund within six to eight weeks if duplicate payment has been identified.

title: Is the acceptance fee refundable or transferable?
text:
Question: Is the acceptance fee refundable or transferable?
Answer: The acceptance fee for the programme, which is stated in the offer letter, is non-refundable or non-transferable except under the following circumstances:

title: How should I submit my application?
text:
Question: How should I submit my application?
Answer: Please apply online through our website and read the 'Notes for Applicants' carefully before submitting your application. To create an application account by inputting your personal information and then note down your electronic ID, application number and password so that you can access your application account in future. When you have finished entering the data in various forms and have uploaded the supporting documents (e.g. transcripts, award certificates), click the 'Pay and Submit' button to proceed with online payment by using VISA, MasterCard, UnionPay*, Alipay HK/ China, Apple Pay, WeChat Pay, Faster Payment System (FPS) or PayMe. Your application will only be processed after the application fee has been paid. An acknowledgement of your application will be shown online immediately after successful payment of the application fee and you will also receive an email message to confirm the submission of your application. *UnionPay service does not support Microsoft EDGE browser. Please use Microsoft IE or Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox.

title: How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted my application for the programme?
text:
Question: How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted my application for the programme?
Answer: Once you have successfully paid your application fee, the programme name will appear under the 'Submitted Applications' section in the application system. In addition, an acknowledgement of your application will be shown online immediately after successful payment of the application fee and you will also receive an email message to confirm the submission of your application. Please ensure your email address is valid and will not filter out emails from the University.

title: What is an application account? How can I access it?
text:
Question: What is an application account? How can I access it?
Answer: An application account is created once you have submitted your application online. Through your application account, you can check your application result, update your correspondence information and upload supporting documents. You can access your application account with the electronic ID and password created at the time of application. Only one application account should be created by each applicant (applicable for multiple programme applications).If more than one application account is created, your application will be disqualified.

title: What should I do if I forget my electronic ID or password to log in to my application account?
text:
Question: What should I do if I forget my electronic ID or password to log in to my application account?
Answer: Please go to the account login page, click 'Forgot Electronic ID or Password' and provide the required information to retrieve your electronic ID or reset your password.

title: Can I submit more than one programme application?
text:
Question: Can I submit more than one programme application?
Answer: You can submit more than one programme application under the same application account. The online application fee per programme choice is HK$600 except for EMBA programmes (P01A, P01B and P12) which is HK$1,000. Please do not create more than one application account. If you receive more than one admission offer from the University, you can choose to enrol in one programme only.

title: Do I need to prioritise my programme choices? What should I do if I submit for more than one programme application?
text:
Question: Do I need to prioritise my programme choices? What should I do if I submit for more than one programme application?
Answer: Applicants are not required to prioritise their programme choices. If you have applied for more than one programme, your programme choices will be given the same priority in our processing. Admission offers, if any, may be made at different times and applicants can indicate their acceptance of an alternative offer by completing the required procedures.

title: I cannot log in to my application account. What should I do?
text:
Question: I cannot log in to my application account. What should I do?
Answer: To log in your application account: Go to the account login page, enter your application number / electronic ID and your password. If you have forgotten your password, select 'Forgot Electronic ID or Password' and provide the following information to retrieve your electronic ID or reset your password: HKID or ID / travel document number Email Date of birth Please note that your application account will be locked if you have not logged in to it for more than 6 months. If you are still unable to access your account, please write to us via tpadmit@cityu.edu.hk and provide us with your full name in English, application number, HKID / ID / passport number (if any) and email address. We will reset your password. For security reasons, you are advised to change your password once you have logged in to your account successfully.

title: Can I submit a late application?
text:
Question: Can I submit a late application?
Answer: Late applications may be accepted if places are still available and it is subject to the discretion of relevant academic unit. You are advised to submit your application as early as possible. For some programmes, applications are considered on a rolling basis. Review of applications will start before the deadline and continue until all places are filled. You are advised to check whether the respective academic units accept late applications before submitting your application.

title: What documents do I need to submit besides the application form?
text:
Question: What documents do I need to submit besides the application form?
Answer: A complete set of application submitted online should include*: an application form; an applicant statement, if applicable; a supplementary form for some programmes, if applicable; the required supporting documents of academic / professional qualifications, English proficiency test result; any other supporting documents required by individual programmes; and successful payment of application fee Submitted documents will not be returned. Original documents (including CityUHK qualifications and public examination results) will need to be submitted/ presented for verification at a later time, upon request by the University. Provision of any false or misleading information as identified by the University before or after the admission offer, or failure to provide the full documentation for verification, will lead to disqualification of the admission application, and any offer or enrolment made will be rescinded. All fees paid will not be refunded. *Except for Postgraduate Certificate in Laws (P45), please do not submit your supporting documents by post until applicants are offered an admission place (if any). Except for the official transcript / English test report (e.g. TOEFL) sent directly from the institution/exam organising unit in a sealed envelope, hard copies of any supporting documents received by the University from applicants will not be opened and will be destroyed upon receipt. Applicants with an admission offer, however, will be required to arrange submission of the required documents by the stipulated deadline for qualification checking. Please read the details.

title: When should I submit the supporting documents? Can I provide additional information after I have submitted my application?
text:
Question: When should I submit the supporting documents? Can I provide additional information after I have submitted my application?
Answer: Document files should be uploaded via your application account before submitting your programme application. Please note that selection decisions will be based on the qualifications set out in your application. Additional information and documents provided to the University after the commencement of the selection process may not be considered as it is subject to the discretion of the relevant academic unit.

title: I will complete my current study by September 2026 but I do not have a transcript / certificate at the time of application. Can I submit an application without the transcript / certificate?
text:
Question: I will complete my current study by September 2026 but I do not have a transcript / certificate at the time of application. Can I submit an application without the transcript / certificate?
Answer: You may submit your application stating your expected graduation date, along with any previous academic results and the latest transcript of your final year of study. Conditional offers may be made to applicants, stipulating successful completion of their current study as a requirement for admission. Applicants are required to arrange submission of the required documents by the stipulated deadline for qualification checking. Please read the details. F12. How can I cancel or remove my online application? I have not paid the application fee. No cancellation of un-submitted applications is required. Your application will not be processed if you have not paid the application fee.

title: After I have submitted my application online, can I correct / update the information entered in the system?
text:
Question: After I have submitted my application online, can I correct / update the information entered in the system?
Answer: Once you have submitted your application, the system will disable the data entry function. For changes of your postal / email address and telephone number, however, you may update your record online at any time via the 'Personal Information' section of your application account. For other amendments, please notify the Chow Yei Ching School of Graduate Studies in writing and provide us with your full name, application number and programme choice(s) for identification. You may be asked to provide documentary proof for making these changes. Please note that the selection results will be based on the information provided at the time of your application. Additional, updated or amended information provided to the University after the commencement of the selection process may or may not be considered and it is subject to the discretion of the relevant academic unit.

title: Will the academic unit know that I have applied for admission to more than one programme?
text:
Question: Will the academic unit know that I have applied for admission to more than one programme?
Answer: Particulars (e.g. personal information and qualifications) relating to your application are shared among the programmes which you have applied for, except for information specific to individual programmes.

title: How will the information I provide in the application be used?
text:
Question: How will the information I provide in the application be used?
Answer: The information you provided in your application, including your HKID card / passport / travel document number, examination results and qualifications, will be used for the following purposes: as a basis for various types of processing in relation to your application; as a basis for the selection of applicants for admission to the programmes offered by the University and/or in collaboration with other institutions; for other opportunities relating to funding schemes / awards by the University or other parties; and for other relevant or related purposes; to obtain, from the relevant examination authority, assessment body or academic institution in Hong Kong and elsewhere, information about your examination results, records of studies and/or professional qualifications if deemed appropriate; to carry out checks on your applications, and any records of studies at the University and other institutions in Hong Kong and elsewhere if deemed appropriate; to carry out checks on other information mentioned in your application (e.g. employment records, professional qualifications, etc.) with the relevant party / parties if deemed appropriate; to transfer relevant data to i) the student record system of the University, ii) partner institutions under collaboration arrangements and/or other parties supporting financial schemes / awards, if applicable upon your successful enrolment at the University;

title: If I do not have VISA, MasterCard or UnionPay, how can I pay for the application fee?
text:
Question: If I do not have VISA, MasterCard or UnionPay, how can I pay for the application fee?
Answer: The application fee for online application can be paid by using VISA, MasterCard, UnionPay*, Alipay HK/ China, Apple Pay, WeChat Pay, Faster Payment System (FPS) or PayMe. You may ask a holder of the said payment means (e.g. a friend or relative) to make the payment for you. *UnionPay service does not support Microsoft EDGE browser. Please use Microsoft IE or Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox.

title: I am interested in CityUHK master's programmes. Do I need to submit an application through an educational agency?
text:
Question: I am interested in CityUHK master's programmes. Do I need to submit an application through an educational agency?
Answer: There is NO need to submit an application through any agency or company. Applications can be submitted directly by applicants via our online application system.Please refer to the programme information and application procedures for details about individual programmes and how to submit your application. CityUHK does not entrust any agencies or companies in Chinese Mainland, for recruitment of postgraduate students or conducting any information / consultation sessions of postgraduate studies. G - Application Result

title: When will the application result be announced?
text:
Question: When will the application result be announced?
Answer: Depending on the programme you have applied for, applications may be reviewed by the academic units as soon as they are received. Admission offers are mostly made next year between January and June. The selection process is normally completed by the end of June for non-local applicants, or by the end of July for local applicants. Late announcement of application results may be due to the following reasons: the application was submitted after the application deadline; or examination results relevant to the evaluation are still pending; or a marginal case for which the selection decision depends on the enrolment situation. Please contact the programme offering academic unit for any further enquiries.

title: How will I know my application result?
text:
Question: How will I know my application result?
Answer: You should check your application result online via your application account from time to time by accessing the account with the electronic ID and password created at the time of your application. If you are successful in your application, you will receive an email notification reminding you to check the application account to view and download the offer letter and to complete the required procedures for accepting the offer. No hard copy will be issued. Please ensure that your email address is valid and will not filter out any emails from the University.

title: Can I apply for credit transfer if I am offered admission?
text:
Question: Can I apply for credit transfer if I am offered admission?
Answer: Applications for credit transfer for coursework completed prior to entry to the University must be made in the first semester following the student’s admission, before the deadline set by the University. Transferred credits will not be counted in the calculation of the Grade Point Average (GPA) and classification of the award. Credit transfer should be limited to a maximum of 30% of the total credit units for the award being pursued.

title: I received a conditional offer letter from the University. Will I receive a further letter confirming the admission offer after I have fulfilled the conditions?
text:
Question: I received a conditional offer letter from the University. Will I receive a further letter confirming the admission offer after I have fulfilled the conditions?
Answer: Applicants with a conditional offer are required to provide the official documentation certifying fulfilment of condition(s) before the deadline indicated in the offer letter. If you have submitted the required documents and are considered as having fulfilled ALL the conditions set out in the offer letter, your online application status will be marked as 'Condition Met' under 'Meeting Offer Condition(s)', but your application result will NOT be changed from 'Conditional Offer' to 'Firm Offer'. No separate notification or firm offer letter will be issued to you. If you fail to provide the required documents, your admission offer will be withdrawn and any enrolment made in the programme will also be rescinded.

title: If I am offered a place at your University this year, can I defer admission to a year later?
text:
Question: If I am offered a place at your University this year, can I defer admission to a year later?
Answer: The admission offer is valid for the semester indicated in the offer letter and it will lapse if you fail to enrol in the indicated semester for admission.

title: I have accepted an offer of admission. When will I receive information on enrolment, class schedules and course registration?
text:
Question: I have accepted an offer of admission. When will I receive information on enrolment, class schedules and course registration?
Answer: Applicants will be notified of enrolment arrangements by email normally in mid-July after they have accepted the admission offer and settled the acceptance fee. Applicants given a conditional offer will normally be notified of enrolment after condition(s) of offer is / are met. Students will be notified of the arrangements for the class schedule and course registration near the start of the semester.

title: If I decline the admission offer this year, will it affect my application next year?
text:
Question: If I decline the admission offer this year, will it affect my application next year?
Answer: Applications are reviewed based on the information submitted in the year of admission, without reference to any previous unsuccessful applications.

title: Can I appeal if I am denied admission?
text:
Question: Can I appeal if I am denied admission?
Answer: We strongly discourage appeals unless you can provide significant new information in writing for our consideration. Our admission decisions are based on a careful and thorough review of each application. It would be highly unusual for the University to reverse such decisions.

title: What do I need to do if I am offered admission?
text:
Question: What do I need to do if I am offered admission?
Answer: Successful applicants can view their offer letters via their application account online with the 'Notes for Applicants on Acceptance of Offer' (the 'Notes') and other information about admission to the University. Please follow the instructions on the 'Notes' to confirm your acceptance of offer by the specified deadline.

title: I logged in to the application account and noted that Firm Offer' is stated in the 'Application Result'. Does it mean that I was accepted for the programme? And, if so, what are the next steps for enrolment in the programme?
text:
Question: I logged in to the application account and noted that Firm Offer' is stated in the 'Application Result'. Does it mean that I was accepted for the programme? And, if so, what are the next steps for enrolment in the programme?
Answer: 'Firm Offer' means you have been offered admission to the specified programme and an official offer letter has been uploaded to your application account. Please read the offer letter and 'Notes for Applicants on Acceptance of Offer' carefully and follow the instructions given to confirm your acceptance of the offer by the specified deadline. No hardcopy of the offer letter will be issued.

title: I received an offer letter and have accepted the offer and paid online. Could you please acknowledge receipt of my payment of the acceptance fee?
text:
Question: I received an offer letter and have accepted the offer and paid online. Could you please acknowledge receipt of my payment of the acceptance fee?
Answer: Please note that upon your indication of your acceptance of offer and pay the acceptance fee online, your application account will be updated accordingly. You may check the updated information under 'Applicant Reply' through your application account.

title: Should attachment of a Cover Sheet for Transcript Submission to the transcript be required after acceptance of offer?
text:
Question: Should attachment of a Cover Sheet for Transcript Submission to the transcript be required after acceptance of offer?
Answer: Please note that the Cover Sheet for Transcript Submission is not required. For details, please read the Information on Supporting Documents - After Offer Acceptance. G13. I received an offer of admission from the University and started to submit my application for a student visa. I want to know if the University will act as the sponsor for my visa application and who should I contact? (applicable to non-local applicants) Our student visa team of the Admissions Office (ADMO) coordinates matters relating to student visa / entry permit applications. Please contact them for assistance. Phone (852) 3442 5652 Email admovisa@cityu.edu.hk Website http://www.cityu.edu.hk/admo/non-local-students/visa/student-visa For information about student visa/entry permit, please visit the official website of the Immigration Department of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR). H - Living Costs and Accommodation (applicable to non-local applicants)

title: Can you give me an estimated cost for studying at CityUHK as a non-local student?
text:
Question: Can you give me an estimated cost for studying at CityUHK as a non-local student?
Answer: For government-funded programmes, the tuition fee for non-local students is HKD190,000 per annum for students admitted in 2026 (the tuition fee may be adjusted if there are changes to the tuition fee set by the HKSAR Government). For non-government funded programmes, the tuition fees are charged on a credit unit basis and vary for individual programmes and the fees are set at a higher level. For details of our tuition fees, please refer to the online programme information. Student Accommodation Please visit our Student Residence Office (SRO) websites on Master’s degree students and/or Off-campus Accommodation for the latest information. For enquiries, please contact SRO: Ma On Shan (MOS) Compound Email: sropga.mos@cityu.edu.hk Kowloon Tong (KLNT) Compound Email: sropga@cityu.edu.hk Cost of Living The cost of living varies depending on individuals lifestyle choices. The average cost of living in Hong Kong (excluding tuition and accommodation fees) is estimated to be HKD50,000 (approx. USD6,400) per 2 semesters, including meals, transport, laundry and education expenses such as books and stationery. Students may also need to budget for overseas learning activities and emergency medical expenses, etc. I - Visiting Study (applicable to non-local applicants)

title: Can I apply for admission as a visiting student?
text:
Question: Can I apply for admission as a visiting student?
Answer: If you are interested in taking courses on a short-term basis (less than one academic year), you may apply for admission as a visiting student. For details, please refer to the visiting postgraduate students website. About CityUHK Introduction

title: Why CityUHK
text:
Question: Why CityUHK

title: What distinguishes research from taught programmes?
text:
Question: What distinguishes research from taught programmes?
Answer: Research programmes (e.g. MPhil, PhD) (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/programmes/study-options#rpg) primarily focus on research work and require students to have a strong research background. Besides coursework, students must submit a thesis and pass an oral defence conducted by the Thesis Assessment Committee to graduate. Taught programmes (e.g. MA, MBA, MSc, MSSc) (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/programmes/study-options#tpg) focus on coursework and usually don't require a thesis. Students complete a specified number of courses to fulfil the credit requirement for graduation.

title: What are the normative and maximum study periods?
text:
Question: What are the normative and maximum study periods?
Answer: Normative study period: The standard duration for students to complete their programmes according to the prescribed study scheme. Most students graduate within this timeframe. Maximum study period: The longest duration allowed for students to complete their programmes. Exceeding this limit requires discontinuation of studies, with exceptions granted by the Graduate Council only in highly exceptional circumstances. Programmes may have varying normative and maximum study periods. For details, refer to Study Options (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/programmes/study-options)

title: When does the new academic year start?
text:
Question: When does the new academic year start?
Answer: For postgraduate programmes, the academic year typically begins in August and ends in July of the following year.

title: Can I enrol in multiple programmes simultaneously?
text:
Question: Can I enrol in multiple programmes simultaneously?
Answer: Students must obtain advance approval from the Graduate Division, Faculty, and Graduate Council to register for multiple programmes at CUHK or other institutions. Violators may face discontinuation of studies.

title: Is it permissible to work while studying?
text:
Question: Is it permissible to work while studying?
Answer: Full-time postgraduate students are not allowed to take up any full-time employment, paid or unpaid, during term time, except as stated in Employment section of the Code of Practice. Postgraduate Studentship (PGS) recipients may take up research or studies related remunerative engagements (full-time or part-time) within their normative study period. Students will need to take in-residence leave and relinquish or deduct the PGS during the period concerned. Prior endorsement from their thesis Supervisor and Head of the Graduate Division, and approval of the Dean of Graduate School are required. PGS holders may take up part-time on-campus employment up to 50 hours per academic year. If the engagement concerned is requested by the students’ Graduate Division, the ceiling will be a total of 100 hours per academic year. The approval authority rests with the Head of the Graduate Division. However, applications that exceed the ceiling should be submitted to the Dean of Graduate School for prior approval.

title: Where can I find information about on-campus accommodation?
text:
Question: Where can I find information about on-campus accommodation?
Answer: Visit the Postgraduate Halls website (https://www.pgh.cuhk.edu.hk) or contact them directly at enquiry@pgh.cuhk.edu.hk for more information. Application for Admission

title: Are there differences between local and non-local applicant processes?
text:
Question: Are there differences between local and non-local applicant processes?
Answer: Application procedures (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/how-to-apply) and requirements (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/requirements)

title: Can bachelor's degree holders apply for doctoral programmes?
text:
Question: Can bachelor's degree holders apply for doctoral programmes?
Answer: Most doctoral programmes require a master's degree. Some Graduate Divisions offer MPhil–PhD Programmes

title: Can final-year undergraduates apply?
text:
Question: Can final-year undergraduates apply?
Answer: Yes, final year undergraduate can apply for postgraduate programmes admission. Please submit your latest official transcript. If accepted, provide an official final transcript and degree certificate upon admission.

title: Is meeting English Language Requirements mandatory?
text:
Question: Is meeting English Language Requirements mandatory?
Answer: Yes. Refer to Admissions Requirements (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/requirements) for details. Some programmes may have specific English language requirements for admission, you are advised to visit the individual programme pages (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/programmes) for more details.

title: Is IELTS for UKVI (Academic) accepted?
text:
Question: Is IELTS for UKVI (Academic) accepted?
Answer: Yes, it fulfils the English language requirements.

title: Do you accept TOEFL "MyBest Score" or IELTS "One Skill Retake"?
text:
Question: Do you accept TOEFL "MyBest Score" or IELTS "One Skill Retake"?
Answer: No, we only accept the test result from each individual attempt.

title: Is CET6 score accepted for English requirements?
text:
Question: Is CET6 score accepted for English requirements?
Answer: No, CET6 is not accepted. Refer to English language requirements (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/requirements) for alternatives.

title: What supporting documents are needed?
text:
Question: What supporting documents are needed?
Answer: Applicants must submit the required supporting documents listed in Documents Required (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/documents-required) . After submitting your application, upload the documents to the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) by the programme deadline.

title: Are hardcopy application forms available?
text:
Question: Are hardcopy application forms available?
Answer: No, in response to the University's sustainability initiatives, no hardcopy application forms are available. Applicants must submit their applications online via the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx)

title: Can I apply for multiple programmes with one application?
text:
Question: Can I apply for multiple programmes with one application?
Answer: You can apply for multiple programmes, but you must submit separate applications and pay the application fees for each programme. The paid application fee is non-refundable and non-transferable. If admitted to multiple programmes, you can only enrol in one. All fees paid to the previous programmes will not be refunded or transferred.

title: When should I apply?
text:
Question: When should I apply?
Answer: Programmes accept applications from September onwards and deadlines vary for each programme. For the Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme (HKPFS) (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/scholarships-fees/hkpfs) , the deadline is 1 December 2025 at 11:59pm (Hong Kong Time). Check Application Deadlines (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/application-deadline) relevant programme pages (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/programmes) for details.

title: What's the application fee and payment method?
text:
Question: What's the application fee and payment method?
Answer: The application fee is HK$500 per programme (except for MBA, MBA in Finance and MSc in Finance). The fee is non-refundable and non-transferable. Payment should be made online by credit card when submitting the application.

title: How do I check my application and payment status?
text:
Question: How do I check my application and payment status?
Answer: Log in to the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) to check your application status and pay the application fee by credit card. The application fee is non-refundable and non-transferable. Financial support for Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students

title: What financial assistance is available to RPg students? How to apply?
text:
Question: What financial assistance is available to RPg students? How to apply?
Answer: Full-time research postgraduate programme applicants are automatically considered for Postgraduate Studentships (PGS) (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/scholarships-fees/studentships) by their programme's Graduate Division. No separate application is needed. Local full-time students in UGC-funded RPg programmes are eligible for the Government's Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/scholarships-fees/scholarships#waiver) Students. This non-mean-tested scheme waives tuition for eligible local students during their normative study period.

title: What are UGC-funded RPg programmes? Do they include part-time programmes?
text:
Question: What are UGC-funded RPg programmes? Do they include part-time programmes?
Answer: All our full-time RPg programmes are UGC-funded, while part-time RPg programmes are not. Full-time RPg students without PGS are considered self-financed. Part-time and self-financed local full-time RPg students are ineligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students.

title: I have an IANG visa. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
text:
Question: I have an IANG visa. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
Answer: Students with any Hong Kong visa type (including student, dependent, and IANG) or entry permit are ineligible for this Scheme.

title: I'm a current local full-time student receiving University PGS. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
text:
Question: I'm a current local full-time student receiving University PGS. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
Answer: To avoid double benefit situations, should the RPg student’s tuition fee be fully waived by any other scheme, then they cannot qualify for the tuition waiver. Should the tuition fee be partially waived, then the RPg students can still qualify for the remaining tuition waiver. Scholarships, awards or stipends, which do not aim to cover the tuition fees, do not affect the waiver.

title: My tuition fee is waived by the University. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
text:
Question: My tuition fee is waived by the University. Am I eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
Answer: Students with fully waived tuition fees are ineligible. However, those with partially waived fees can receive Scheme coverage for the remaining portion.

title: If I receive the Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme or other scholarships, am I still eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
text:
Question: If I receive the Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme or other scholarships, am I still eligible for the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
Answer: Local recipients of the Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme or other scholarships are eligible if their scholarships, awards, or stipends don't cover tuition fees.

title: As a Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme recipient, will I receive both the CUHK Vice-Chancellor HKPFS Scholarship and the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
text:
Question: As a Hong Kong PhD Fellowship Scheme recipient, will I receive both the CUHK Vice-Chancellor HKPFS Scholarship and the Tuition Waiver Scheme for Local Research Postgraduate (RPg) Students?
Answer: The CUHK Vice-Chancellor HKPFS Scholarship include a tuition fee waiver for the recipient’s normative study period. While local recipients enrolled in the UGC-funded RPg full-time programmes also qualify for the Tuition Waiver for Local RPg Students for their normative study period, the waived tuition fees will not translate into additional sums for the local HKPFS awardee to collect. Offer Acceptance and Registration

title: When will I receive my admission offer?
text:
Question: When will I receive my admission offer?
Answer: Admission offers are normally made between November to June of the following year. Applicants can check their application status via the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx)

title: How can I check my offer?
text:
Question: How can I check my offer?
Answer: Applicants can log in to the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) to check their application results and download the Admission Notification.

title: How to Register
text:
Question: How to Register
Answer: for provisional registration. However, please note that your student status is not yet confirmed and you will NOT be allowed to begin your studies until you have fulfilled all the specified condition(s) and have your academic qualification verified by the deadline mentioned in the Admission Notification.

title: I am currently a final-year undergraduate student. Do I need to re-submit an official transcript if the transcript I submitted during my application is final?
text:
Question: I am currently a final-year undergraduate student. Do I need to re-submit an official transcript if the transcript I submitted during my application is final?
Answer: Yes. You are still required to apply for a final official transcript after the completion of your Bachelor’s programme or the announcement of your honours classification (if applicable). Please arrange for the official e-copy or hard copy transcript to be sent directly from your university to the Programme Office (for taught programmes) or the Graduate School (for research programmes).

title: What if I cannot provide documents to fulfil the admission condition(s) by the deadline?
text:
Question: What if I cannot provide documents to fulfil the admission condition(s) by the deadline?
Answer: If you cannot provide document(s) to fulfil the admission condition(s) mentioned in the Admission Notification, your admission offer will be withdrawn. You may seek advice or assistance from the relevant Graduate Division/Programme if necessary. You are also required to have your academic qualifications verified before the admission date

title: Can I defer the admission date?
text:
Question: Can I defer the admission date?
Answer: You may contact your Graduate Division/Programme first to see if you could defer your admission date. If the deferral is allowed, please complete and return the "Application Form for Deferred Admission” available on the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) to the relevant Division/Programme after accepting the admission offer online and making the deposit payment. The admission can be deferred for a maximum of one year from the original admission date.

title: When do I need to settle the deposit after accepting an admission offer?
text:
Question: When do I need to settle the deposit after accepting an admission offer?
Answer: The payment due date for deposit payment is specified in your admission notification. You may refer to the payment advice at the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) for various payment methods as well as other relevant details.

title: Can I defer the acceptance of the admission offer and the deposit payment?
text:
Question: Can I defer the acceptance of the admission offer and the deposit payment?
Answer: You may contact your Graduate Division/Programme to see if your deferral request could be accepted. If it is accepted, you may complete and return the "Application Form for Deferred Acceptance of Admission Offer" at the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) to the respective Division/Programme. Please note that the acceptance of an admission offer cannot be deferred beyond the admission date.

title: How can I settle the tuition fee?
text:
Question: How can I settle the tuition fee?
Answer: The tuition fee is normally settled on a term basis. Students will receive a fee note via email about two weeks before the payment due date. For details, please refer to Notes on Payment of Student Fees (https://www.fno.cuhk.edu.hk/student/student-fees/faq/)

title: How can I update my contact information after accepting the admission offer?
text:
Question: How can I update my contact information after accepting the admission offer?
Answer: After completing the deposit payment and online registration, you may update your contact information at MyCUHK. (https://portal.cuhk.edu.hk/)

title: Where can I get my CU Link (Student Card)?
text:
Question: Where can I get my CU Link (Student Card)?
Answer: For local students, upon completing the online registration and fulfilling all admission condition(s), you can collect your CU Link from the CU Link Card Centre (https://culink.cuhk.edu.hk/collection-of-cu-link-student-card-for-new-postgraduates2/) , on or after your admission date. Non-local students are required to complete online document verification before collecting their CU Link ( Learn more (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/registration/visa-documents-submission) ). Upon completing the online document verification, please contact the CU Link Card Centre (https://culink.cuhk.edu.hk/collection-of-cu-link-student-card-for-new-postgraduates2/) after 7 working days to check the status and collection arrangements. Student Visa/Entry Permit

title: Do I need to apply for a student visa/entry permit to study at CUHK?
text:
Question: Do I need to apply for a student visa/entry permit to study at CUHK?
Answer: Non-local students without the right of abode (https://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/services/right-of-abode-in-hksar.html) right to land (https://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/services/roa/term.html) in Hong Kong MUST obtain a valid visa/entry permit to study at CUHK. Part-time students with another valid visa/entry permit should consult the HKSAR Government's Immigration Department (https://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/index.html) about the need for a student visa/entry permit. Non-local students without a valid visa/entry permit at registration will NOT be allowed to register. Students are reminded that it is your responsibility to ensure you hold a valid visa/entry permit from the Immigration Department at all times during your stay in HKSAR, failing which you may face the risk of being prosecuted for breach of condition of stay.

title: I'm a non-local CUHK undergraduate. If admitted to a CUHK postgraduate programme, should I extend my current student visa or apply for a new one?
text:
Question: I'm a non-local CUHK undergraduate. If admitted to a CUHK postgraduate programme, should I extend my current student visa or apply for a new one?
Answer: As situations vary, please consult the HKSAR Government's Immigration Department (https://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/index.html) (Tel: 852 2824-6111; email: enquiry@immd.gov.hk ) for advice.

title: When should I apply for the student visa/entry permit?
text:
Question: When should I apply for the student visa/entry permit?
Answer: The application process normally takes around 6‐8 weeks. The Immigration Department will take longer time during the summer peak season. It is the student’s responsibility to apply for their visa/entry permit in a timely manner and to apply for extension before its expiry. Admission Date Time to Apply 1 August At least 2 months before commencement of study 1 September At least 2 months before commencement of study 1 January At least 2 months before commencement of study

title: How do I apply for a student visa/entry permit to study at CUHK?
text:
Question: How do I apply for a student visa/entry permit to study at CUHK?
Answer: The Graduate School will assist with your application to the HKSAR Government's Immigration Department (https://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/index.html) upon admission. The handling fee should be settled by credit card on the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) . For details, see Visa and Related Matters for Non-local Students (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/student-portal/visa-for-non-local-students)

title: How long does the visa application process take?
text:
Question: How long does the visa application process take?
Answer: Upon receipt of the duly completed application forms, supporting documents and payment, the Immigration Department takes around 6-8 weeks to process the applications. Chinese Mainland students should allow sufficient time to obtain an Exit-entry Permit for Traveling to and from Hong Kong and Macao (EEP) 《來往港澳通行證》and Relevant Exit Endorsement 《相關赴港簽注》 from the local Public Security Bureau Office 《戶口所在地公安機關出入境管理部門》 where your household registration is kept. Visit the HKSAR Government's Immigration Department (https://www.immd.gov.hk/eng/index.html) website for more information.

title: What documents are needed for the student visa application?
text:
Question: What documents are needed for the student visa application?
Answer: View the document list at Visa Application (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/registration/visa-application)

title: What financial proof is required for the student visa application?
text:
Question: What financial proof is required for the student visa application?
Answer: Full-time research postgraduate students with Postgraduate Studentships (PGS) (https://www.gs.cuhk.edu.hk/admissions/scholarships-fees/studentships)

title: can submit their Letter of Award as financial proof.
text:
Question: can submit their Letter of Award as financial proof.
Answer: Taught postgraduate students should demonstrate sufficient funds to support their living and studies in Hong Kong.

title: Where should I submit my student visa application documents?
text:
Question: Where should I submit my student visa application documents?
Answer: Please login to the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) . Navigate to “Apply/Check” under “Visa for Study”, follow the on screen instructions to submit your student visa application.

title: How can I check my student visa/entry permit application status?
text:
Question: How can I check my student visa/entry permit application status?
Answer: Check the status on the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx)

title: How do I activate my student visa/entry permit in Hong Kong?
text:
Question: How do I activate my student visa/entry permit in Hong Kong?
Answer: For Chinese Mainland students: To activate the student visa, you should enter into Hong Kong with the student visa and the Exit-entry Permit for Traveling to and from Hong Kong and Macao (EEP)《來往港澳通行證》with Relevant Exit Endorsement《相關赴港簽注》. For other overseas students: To activate the student visa, you should enter into Hong Kong with the student visa and your passport.

title: Should I keep the landing slip after entering Hong Kong?
text:
Question: Should I keep the landing slip after entering Hong Kong?
Answer: A landing slip that states your personal information, arrival date, conditions and the duration of your stay in Hong Kong will be given to you at the Immigration Counter. Please keep the landing slip and upload the copy, together with copies of the visa label and travel document, to the Online Application System for Postgraduate Programmes (https://www.gradsch.cuhk.edu.hk/OnlineApp/login_email.aspx) to fulfil the admission condition.
