title: Will I receive the original copy of the Admission Offer Letter?
text:
Question: Will I receive the original copy of the Admission Offer Letter?
Answer: The Admission Offer Letter will be uploaded to the Online Application System (https://iss.hkbu.edu.hk/amsappl_pg/welcome.jsf) (Application Details --> Application Status). Please download it from the system and print it out for reference (see Screen below). Only soft copy is provided. [Click to Enlarge]

title: How can my conditional offer be confirmed?
text:
Question: How can my conditional offer be confirmed?
Answer: Please refer to the Offer Condition(s) as listed on Online Application System (https://iss.hkbu.edu.hk/amsappl_pg/welcome.jsf) (Application Details -> Offer Condition) and complete the Actions for fulfilment of offer condition(s) by the deadline(s) as shown. Once the Actions for fulfilment of offer condition(s) has been completed and checked by the university, the Fulfilled column will be updated as "Yes". It normally takes about one month for the University to check and update the status. If there is any issue, you will be contacted by email. [Click to Enlarge]

title: What should I do if I cannot meet the offer condition(s) by the given due date?
text:
Question: What should I do if I cannot meet the offer condition(s) by the given due date?
Answer: If you fail to achieve the required degree award and/or obtain the required English test score and/or other test score by the due date(s) as specified in the offer conditions (OAS -> Application Details -> Offer Condition), please write to the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section at hkbu_tpg@hkbu.edu.hk and attach relevant and sufficient documentary proof to your email for our consideration. A refund of the paid admission confirmation fee (with an administration fee of HK$5,000 deducted) may be granted on a discretionary basis.

title: What is an official transcript?
text:
Question: What is an official transcript?
Answer: An official transcript is a certified statement of your academic record which lists all the courses and grades that you achieved during your undergraduate and/or postgraduate studies. The official transcript is issued by the institution of your awarded degree. Please refer to the “Guideline” on Online Application System (https://iss.hkbu.edu.hk/amsappl_pg/welcome.jsf) (Application Details -> Offer Condition) for details.

title: When should I apply for the official transcript(s)?
text:
Question: When should I apply for the official transcript(s)?
Answer: To allow sufficient time for validation of your academic qualification(s), please arrange the official transcript(s) to be sent to the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section as soon as possible. In the case that you need more time to make the arrangement, please ensure that the official transcript(s) shall reach the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section no later than 31 July or other due date as specified on your Admission Offer Letter. Your admission offer will be cancelled if we are unable to receive and validate your academic qualification(s) by the given due date.

title: My official transcript issued by the awarding institution is not in English. Should I provide a translated version?
text:
Question: My official transcript issued by the awarding institution is not in English. Should I provide a translated version?
Answer: If your official transcript(s) is in a language other than Chinese or English, please also provide a certified translated copy of the official transcript(s) in English.

title: Would the University accept my transcript (student copy) for serving the purpose of providing the official transcript?
text:
Question: Would the University accept my transcript (student copy) for serving the purpose of providing the official transcript?
Answer: No, student copy is not accepted. You should arrange with the awarding institution(s) to post the official transcript(s) in a sealed and stamped envelope directly to the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section.

title: How can I know if the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section has received my official transcript(s) and/or other outstanding document(s)?
text:
Question: How can I know if the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section has received my official transcript(s) and/or other outstanding document(s)?
Answer: Please refer to the Offer Condition status on Online Application System (https://iss.hkbu.edu.hk/amsappl_pg/welcome.jsf) (Application Details -> Offer Condition). Once the Actions for fulfilment of offer condition(s) has been completed and checked by the university, the Fulfilled column will be updated as "Yes". It normally takes about one month for the University to check and update the status. If there is any issue, you will be contacted by email. [Click to Enlarge]

title: What should I do if I am unable to arrange with the awarding institution(s) to post the official transcript by the due date?
text:
Question: What should I do if I am unable to arrange with the awarding institution(s) to post the official transcript by the due date?
Answer: The University may consider deferring the due date for you to submit the official transcript(s). Please write to the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section at hkbu_tpg@hkbu.edu.hk and provide the following information for the University’s consideration: 1. An explanation stating the reason(s) for the delay (please quote your full name and application number for reference); 2. State the earliest possible date for the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section to receive the official transcript(s); and 3. Attach relevant supporting document(s). The Taught Postgraduate Studies Section shall inform you of the result through email in one week upon receipt of your application. Please note that if you do not receive the approval to defer your official transcript’s submission and we cannot receive your official transcript(s) by 31 July or by the due date as specified on your Admission Offer Letter, your admission offer will be cancelled.

title: Do I need to apply for a student visa?
text:
Question: Do I need to apply for a student visa?
Answer: Please refer to your Admission Offer Letter (see Screen below) to check whether you are required to apply for a student visa. [Click to Enlarge]

title: I have a valid permit issued by the Immigration Department. Do I still need to apply for student visa?
text:
Question: I have a valid permit issued by the Immigration Department. Do I still need to apply for student visa?
Answer: Please refer to your Admission Offer Letter to check whether you are required to apply for a student visa (please refer to Q10 above). Non-local students should possess a valid visa/entry permit to stay and study in Hong Kong during the entire study period of the enrolled study programme. Students may be required to provide the documentary proof of their eligibility to stay and study in Hong Kong when necessary. Without a valid visa/entry permit, students will be required to officially withdraw from the programme.

title: When and how should I apply for a student visa?
text:
Question: When and how should I apply for a student visa?
Answer: You will receive an invitation email for activating the account at the University Student Visa Application System (https://gs-visa.hkbu.edu.hk/) about one week after you have settled the admission confirmation fee. Please wait for this email before action. As the Immigration Department of Hong Kong Special Administrative Region normally takes at least two months to process a visa application, you should complete your visa application via the University Student Visa Application System (https://gs-visa.hkbu.edu.hk/) starting from 1 April to 31 July. To better prepare for your student visa application, please carefully read through the application flow chart (https://ar.hkbu.edu.hk/f/upload/5260/visa_flow_tpg.jpg) and check the documents required (https://ar.hkbu.edu.hk/tpg-studies/faq/tpg-studies/prospective-student/apply-for-student-visa)

title: I have not received my degree certificate. Can I apply for student visa first?
text:
Question: I have not received my degree certificate. Can I apply for student visa first?
Answer: Yes. Please submit your student visa application via the University Student Visa Application System (https://gs-visa.hkbu.edu.hk/) . You may upload a copy of the degree certificate to the University Student Visa Application System (https://gs-visa.hkbu.edu.hk/) by the due date specified on the Admission Offer Letter.

title: I have not applied for Exit-Entry Permit for Travelling to and from Hong Kong and Macao (往來港澳通行證) (EEP), or my EEP has not been renewed. Can I apply for student visa first?
text:
Question: I have not applied for Exit-Entry Permit for Travelling to and from Hong Kong and Macao (往來港澳通行證) (EEP), or my EEP has not been renewed. Can I apply for student visa first?
Answer: Yes. EEP is not a required document for student visa application.

title: I have accepted the admission offer and paid the admission confirmation fee; however, I cannot register at the University Student Visa Application System. What should I do?
text:
Question: I have accepted the admission offer and paid the admission confirmation fee; however, I cannot register at the University Student Visa Application System. What should I do?
Answer: Once you have accepted the admission offer and paid the admission confirmation fee, it takes around one week for the University to update your status. Upon confirmation of your status, you will receive an invitation email for activating your personal account at the University Student Visa Application System (https://gs-visa.hkbu.edu.hk/) for visa application. For first time activation, please do it through the email link. You need your student number (see Screen below) and the email address (i.e. the records in the Online Application System) for activating your account at the University Student Visa Application System (https://gs-visa.hkbu.edu.hk/) [Click to Enlarge]

title: How can I know if the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section has received my original Form ID995A (Application for Entry for Study in Hong Kong)?
text:
Question: How can I know if the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section has received my original Form ID995A (Application for Entry for Study in Hong Kong)?
Answer: The Taught Postgraduate Studies Section will process visa applications in one month upon receipt of the original Form ID995A (sample forms for reference: Mainland (https://ar.hkbu.edu.hk/f/upload/5260/Mainland_visaapp_sample.pdf) Overseas (https://ar.hkbu.edu.hk/f/upload/5260/Guideline_for_student_visa_application__overseas_.pdf) ) and relevant supporting documents. The University will contact you by email in case any document is missing.

title: How can I check my visa application status?
text:
Question: How can I check my visa application status?
Answer: You may log in to the Online Application System (https://iss.hkbu.edu.hk/amsappl_pg/welcome.jsf) (Application Details --> Student Visa) (see Screen below). The submission date of your application from Taught Postgraduate Studies Section to the Hong Kong Immigration Department, and the sent date of the notification email from Taught Postgraduate Studies Section to you containing your Application Reference Number (ARN), will be timely updated for your reference. [Click to Enlarge]

title: How will I receive my student visa label?
text:
Question: How will I receive my student visa label?
Answer: After the issuance by the Immigration Department ("ImmD") of Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR), the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section will send the Application Reference Number (ARN) to your email address for you to download your "e-Visa" through the GovHK website or the ImmD Mobile Application.

title: How do I activate my student visa in Hong Kong?
text:
Question: How do I activate my student visa in Hong Kong?
Answer: You should present your visa/entry permit label and travel document at the Immigration Counter upon you arrival to Hong Kong. Please see in the table below of the documents required for entry to Hong Kong. Please make sure that the Immigration Officer issues a landing slip which bears your English name, travel document number, name of the University, arrival date to Hong Kong, and conditions and limit of stay in Hong Kong. You will not be allowed to register for studies at the University if you cannot show the landing slip on the Registration Day. International Students Students from Macao Students from China Travel Document Passport Visit Permit for Resident of Macao to HKSAR (澳門居民往來香港特別行政區旅遊證) Exit-entry Permit (EEP) for Travelling to and from Hong Kong and Macao (中華人民共和國往来港澳通行證) Visa/entry permit label To be affixed onto a blank page of your Passport for presentation to the Immigration Officer at the Immigration Counter upon your arrival to Hong Kong. To be affixed onto a blank page of your Visit Permit for Resident of Macao to HKSAR for presentation to the Immigration Officer at the Immigration Counter upon your arrival to Hong Kong.

title: How to upload my photo for my student card?
text:
Question: How to upload my photo for my student card?
Answer: You will receive an invitation email for activating the account at the University Student Card Photo Upload System (https://arphoto.hkbu.edu.hk/) about one week after you have settled the admission confirmation fee. Please wait for this email before action. After receiving the invitation email, you can access the University Student Card Photo Upload System (https://arphoto.hkbu.edu.hk/) with your application number ("TXX" + last 6 digits of the number) and the email address you registered at the Online Application System (https://iss.hkbu.edu.hk/amsappl_pg/welcome.jsf)

title: I fail to upload my photo to the University Student Card Photo Upload System. What should I do?
text:
Question: I fail to upload my photo to the University Student Card Photo Upload System. What should I do?
Answer: There are specific requirements for uploading the photo. Before you do so, please check if: 1. You are using Internet Explorer to access the University Student Card Photo Upload System. 2. Your photo is in JPEG format, with a file size between 200KB - 2MB. 3. You attempt to upload the photo for no more than five times per account. If you have any questions, please write to the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section at hkbu_tpg@hkbu.edu.hk, together with the screen capture and/or the error message for handling. Please be reminded to quote your full name and application number for easy reference.

title: When will I receive the student card?
text:
Question: When will I receive the student card?
Answer: The student card will be distributed to our newly admitted students who have completed the student registration.

title: Can I apply for student accommodation?
text:
Question: Can I apply for student accommodation?
Answer: For information on accommodation, please visit the website of the Office of Student Affairs (http://sa.hkbu.edu.hk/sas/)

title: Where can I obtain the reference list/course materials/class schedule to prepare for my studies?
text:
Question: Where can I obtain the reference list/course materials/class schedule to prepare for my studies?
Answer: For information about the course materials/reference list, please refer to the programme website. For information about the course enrolment and class schedules, please refer to the Taught Postgraduate Studies Section programme website. Details will also be announced via email before the programme commencement.

title: When will I graduate?
text:
Question: When will I graduate?
Answer: The awarding of a postgraduate degree is on the basis of fulfilment of the graduation requirements and the approval of the Senate. Students having all grades finalised and meeting the graduation requirements will be recommended for graduation by the department/programme for approval by the Senate via the Taught Postgraduate Studies Committee and Quality Assurance Committee. Please click here (https://ar.hkbu.edu.hk/graduate-services/commencement/diploma-issuance-and-collection-schedule) for the Schedules for Graduation and Diploma Issuance.

title: What happens if the University cancels the programme after I have accepted the offer and paid the fees?
text:
Question: What happens if the University cancels the programme after I have accepted the offer and paid the fees?
Answer: The University reserves the right to cancel any programmes in which case all fees paid will be refunded.

title: When will the application information be available?
text:
Question: When will the application information be available?
Answer: Please note that application information for admission to most taught postgraduate programmes will usually be available from October/November onwards every year. Some postgraduate programmes will be open for application earlier. Please stay tune to the announcement of individual faculties/departments.

title: Where can I obtain the application information?
text:
Question: Where can I obtain the application information?
Answer: Please visit our website for information about admission requirements, programme list, programme information, syllabus for each programme, application procedure, supporting document etc.

title: Where can I obtain information on PhD/MPhil programmes?
text:
Question: Where can I obtain information on PhD/MPhil programmes?
Answer: Please contact the Graduate School for inquiries about Research Postgraduate Programmes. Application

title: How can I submit my application?
text:
Question: How can I submit my application?
Answer: You can apply for our taught postgraduate programmes via the online application system by clicking the "Apply Now" button in the programme page. If you cannot click the button, that means application is not open yet. Please wait until the Department/Faculty concerned announces the latest programme details, which are usually be available from October/November onwards.

title: Can I apply to more than one taught postgraduate programme at HKU?
text:
Question: Can I apply to more than one taught postgraduate programme at HKU?
Answer: Yes, it is possible for you to apply to multiple programmes at HKU. You can apply under different usernames on the application system and pay the application fee (HK$600) for each programme that you wish to apply to. You can only enrol in one programme, even if you receive offers from other programmes.

title: Can I apply for admission if i am still in my last year of study?
text:
Question: Can I apply for admission if i am still in my last year of study?
Answer: You do not need to have graduated from your bachelor's degree programme at the time of application. However, you must obtain a bachelor's degree on or before August 31 of your admission year.

title: Is concurrent registration acceptable?
text:
Question: Is concurrent registration acceptable?
Answer: Applicants are required to provide proof of withdrawal from any other programme of study before confirming an offer. A breach of this regulation may result in discontinuation of studies at HKU.

title: Am I a local or a non-local applicant?
text:
Question: Am I a local or a non-local applicant?
Answer: For definition of local students, please visit Joint University Programmes Admissions System (JUPAS) website here

title: How can I retrieve my application?
text:
Question: How can I retrieve my application?
Answer: If you have logged out your account before submission, please click here to retrieve and re-login to your account.

title: Why can't log in to my application account?
text:
Question: Why can't log in to my application account?
Answer: An application account is only valid for 4 weeks, or up until the application deadline, whichever is earlier. You cannot log into your application account after this period.

title: Whom should I reach out to for guidance when completing the online application form?
text:
Question: Whom should I reach out to for guidance when completing the online application form?
Answer: For any assistance or inquiries regarding the completion of the online application form, please contact the programme office. English Language Requirement

title: If the medium of instruction for my degree is in English, do I still need to take the English Proficiency test?
text:
Question: If the medium of instruction for my degree is in English, do I still need to take the English Proficiency test?
Answer: You are not required to take the English proficiency test if you obtained a master's/bachelor's degree from an institution where the medium of instruction was English. Please check with the relevant Faculty to determine if higher English competency is required for certain programmes. Fee, Payment & Refund

title: Can my application fee be waived?
text:
Question: Can my application fee be waived?
Answer: The application fee cannot be waived. However, you may wish to check with the programme office for scholarships which cover the application fee.

title: What can I do if I have problems in settling the application fee on the online application system?
text:
Question: What can I do if I have problems in settling the application fee on the online application system?
Answer: If you encounter problems with the online payment, please try the following: Use browsers such as, Internet Explorer (IE) or Edge instead of Chrome or Safari and clear all the temporary files and cookies before submitting the payment. Ensure not to open different browsers and/or log in to different application accounts at the same time. Contact the credit card issuing bank to check if you need to obtain authorization from the bank before making an online transaction. Attempt to use another credit card.

title: How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted the application?
text:
Question: How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted the application?
Answer: Upon successful payment of the application fee and submission of an application, the applicant will receive an application number and an email notification within 48 hours.

title: What should I do if the application fee has been deducted but the application is not submitted successfully?
text:
Question: What should I do if the application fee has been deducted but the application is not submitted successfully?
Answer: If the application fee has been deducted but the system indicates that the application was not submitted successfully, there are a few steps you can take to resolve the issue. First, please note that an application number will only be generated if the application is submitted successfully, and the payment will be deducted based on that application number. To resolve the issue, please follow these steps: 1. Re-login to the system and try submitting the payment again. Continue doing so until you receive an application number from the system. 2. Before submitting the payment, it is advisable to remove all temporary files and cookies from your browser. 3. Ensure that you do not open different browsers or log in to different application accounts (if you have any) simultaneously. Please keep in mind that we can only charge once per application number. The funds for both successful and unsuccessful attempts at application fee payments are held by the card-issuing bank, not the University. The bank will release the balance held for the unsuccessful payment attempt(s) at a later time, but the duration of these holds depends on the policy of the card-issuing bank.

title: If I cancel/withdraw my application, can I get a refund of my application fee?
text:
Question: If I cancel/withdraw my application, can I get a refund of my application fee?
Answer: The application fee is non-refundable and non-transferable once submitted.

title: Is the deposit payment refundable and transferable?
text:
Question: Is the deposit payment refundable and transferable?
Answer: Deposit is non-refundable unless you do not meet the conditions specified in the offer of acceptance. For details, please contact the relevant programme office. After Applying

title: What should I do if I forget my username and password?
text:
Question: What should I do if I forget my username and password?
Answer: If you forget your password, click “Forget password” on the login page and reset your password. If you forget your username, please contact the relevant faculty or programme office.

title: I submitted my application successfully but still received an account reminder email. What should I do?
text:
Question: I submitted my application successfully but still received an account reminder email. What should I do?
Answer: This email serves as a reminder that you have created an account for online application without submission. You can let the application account expire if you no longer wish to submit another application.

title: How can I submit the supporting documents?
text:
Question: How can I submit the supporting documents?
Answer: The ways to submit supporting documents may vary by programme. Please refer to "Supporting Documents" of relevant programmes.

title: I cannot upload my documents on the online application system. What should I do?
text:
Question: I cannot upload my documents on the online application system. What should I do?
Answer: Please note that you can only upload your supporting documents before the application deadline or within four weeks of your account creation. If the uploading period has expired, or the file size is too large, you can contact the relevant programme office for advice.

title: What should I do if I wish to add/update my referee information?
text:
Question: What should I do if I wish to add/update my referee information?
Answer: You can email the updated referee information to respective programme office.

title: Do I need to post the original/certified true copy of the supporting documents after submitting an application?
text:
Question: Do I need to post the original/certified true copy of the supporting documents after submitting an application?
Answer: For most programmes, posting documents is not required at the application stage. Should an applicant be considered for admission, he/she will receive an email notification from the programme office to submit the original documents. For details, please refer to the "Supporting Documents" of relevant programmes.

title: Can I change the programme after the submission of application?
text:
Question: Can I change the programme after the submission of application?
Answer: The application form once submitted is non-transferrable.

title: What should I do if I wish to change my personal information on the system?
text:
Question: What should I do if I wish to change my personal information on the system?
Answer: You cannot make any changes in the application form after submission. If you need to make changes to your personal information, please send an email to the relevant programme office and provide your application number and ID/passport number for verification.

title: How can I add or amend information in the application form after the submission?
text:
Question: How can I add or amend information in the application form after the submission?
Answer: You cannot make any changes in the application form after submission. To update or add additional information, please email relevant programme office directly.

title: Will I be invited to attend an admission interview or admission test?
text:
Question: Will I be invited to attend an admission interview or admission test?
Answer: Not all programmes require interviews or tests, please consult the relevant programme office for details.

title: When will the application result be announced? How can I check my application status?
text:
Question: When will the application result be announced? How can I check my application status?
Answer: Please consult the relevant programme office to check your application status or result.

title: How can I obtain a student visa?
text:
Question: How can I obtain a student visa?
Answer: Please note that the processing time for student visa applications is approximately 10-12 weeks. For details, please visit our website: HKU Student Visa Scholarship and Housing

title: Are there any scholarships available for my study in HKU?
text:
Question: Are there any scholarships available for my study in HKU?
Answer: For information on scholarship opportunities, you may contact the scholarship office and programme office for advice.

title: Is postgraduate housing available?
text:
Question: Is postgraduate housing available?
Answer: Residential places on campus for postgraduate students are limited and in high demand. To learn more about eligibility for University housing, please visit the CEDARS website for details and to access the online application.

title: I have received an admission offer online. What should I do next?
text:
Question: I have received an admission offer online. What should I do next?
Answer: Accept the offer online by the offer acceptance deadline. Pay the deposit to confirm your acceptance. Follow through with other important actions as stated in the offer letter. If you are a current/ previous student at the University, make sure you have settled all outstanding payment from your studies in order to proceed to new program registration.

title: How should I notify the University that I have met the offer conditions?
text:
Question: How should I notify the University that I have met the offer conditions?
Answer: Submit relevant documents as proof as early as possible and no later than mid-July for Fall admission, or end December for Spring admission.

title: How can I pay the deposit for accepting the offer?
text:
Question: How can I pay the deposit for accepting the offer?
Answer: Deposit payment instructions will be available after you click “Accept Offer and Pay” in the Online Admission System. Please upload the payment proof by the deadline.

title: I have accepted the offer. What supporting documents should I submit and how?
text:
Question: I have accepted the offer. What supporting documents should I submit and how?
Answer: Check the Document Checklist which will appear after you have accepted the offer online. Refer to Document Requirements for details and tips as below: Arrange official documents to be sent before the deadline stipulated in the offer letter/ Document Checklist. Check the document receipt status in the Document Checklist.

title: What do you mean by official certified true copies?
text:
Question: What do you mean by official certified true copies?
Answer: The University may accept the following as official certified true copies only in cases where the originals of the official documents required to confirm the admission offer cannot be obtained despite all reasonable efforts or provided: (1) Copies officially imprinted, signed and certified by the issuing authority concerned, or (2) Copies that have been certified as certified true copies by qualified professionals including lawyers, certified public accountants or notaries public. (2), however, is not applicable to qualification documents including but not limited to academic transcripts, score reports of public examinations, certification of graduation, and verification reports provided by authorities such as the CHSI. Certified true copies documents should be sent to the HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School (FYTGS) by post. The University reserves the right to decide whether the submitted documents are acceptable. Please refer to the FYTGS website for detailed guidelines on submitting official documents before program registration.

title: My official supporting document is not in English. What should I do?
text:
Question: My official supporting document is not in English. What should I do?
Answer: You should submit the official electronic supporting document which is accompanied by officially certified English translations and is shared by the central unit of your home institution to pgdoc@ust.hk directly via designated secure websites. The translator should sign and stamp on the translation and certify that the document is a true translation of the original. Please visit here for details.

title: What are the University Codes for TOEFL, IELTS, GMAT and GRE?
text:
Question: What are the University Codes for TOEFL, IELTS, GMAT and GRE?
Answer: The codes below apply to all HKUST PG programs: TOEFL (Test of English as a Foreign Language) Score Recipient Code: 0170 Dept Code: any code other than 00, e.g. 01 = Educational Administration IELTS (International English Language Testing System) Please arrange electronic score reports as far as possible Name of department : HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School Name of institution : The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology Address : Room 6418 (Lifts 25/26), Academic Building The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology Clear Water Bay, Kowloon Hong Kong (No postal code is required for Hong Kong)

title: Can I arrange electronic transcript for qualification verification?
text:
Question: Can I arrange electronic transcript for qualification verification?
Answer: Yes, the official electronic transcripts should be shared directly by the central unit of the issuing authorities. Please request your home institution to send official electronic transcripts quoting your application number and applied program to pgdoc@ust.hk. Please note that official electronic transcripts are acceptable only if they are sent from the issuing authorities and retrieved from designated secure websites, which the said practice has been indicating on the website of the issuing authorities.

title: Where should I send the supporting documents to?
text:
Question: Where should I send the supporting documents to?
Answer: Official electronic supporting documents must be sent directly by the issuing authority to pgdoc@ust.hk. Electronic documents which are sent by applicants or other individuals are not accepted. Hard copies of the official documents are generally not accepted.

title: I have submitted my supporting documents, what’s next?
text:
Question: I have submitted my supporting documents, what’s next?
Answer: Login to the application system to check the document receipt status. Normally it takes 7 working days (or 10 working days during the peak in June to August) to update the document receipt status after a document was received. Please also ensure that the documents have been sent to the right e-mail address.

title: I have submitted my visa application online, what’s next?
text:
Question: I have submitted my visa application online, what’s next?
Answer: Send us the required paper documents as soon as possible. Please refer to the Checklist of Documents Required for Visa/ Entry Permit Application for details. You can check your application status in the Visa System.

title: How do I know the status of my student visa/ entry permit application?
text:
Question: How do I know the status of my student visa/ entry permit application?
Answer: Log in to the Visa System which shows the latest visa/ entry permit application status. The University will advise you to download the e-Visa for entry to Hong Kong for study, upon the visa approval. Approved visa/ entry permit must be activated on or before the specified date on the e-Visa.

title: Do I need to activate my student visa/ student status in Hong Kong?
text:
Question: Do I need to activate my student visa/ student status in Hong Kong?
Answer: Click here for guidelines to activate your student visa. These instructions will be sent to you together with your student visa/ entry permit. It is important that you do the steps right.

title: Can I apply for a dependent visa for my family member through HKUST?
text:
Question: Can I apply for a dependent visa for my family member through HKUST?
Answer: You have to make your own arrangement to apply for dependent visa(s) for your family member(s). Also, your dependents are prohibited from taking up employment in Hong Kong unless they have obtained prior permission from the Director of Immigration. Visit here and submit ID997 to the Hong Kong Immigration Department. For more details, check with the Hong Kong Immigration Department [phone: (852) 2824 6111; email: enquiry@immd.gov.hk].

title: Do I need a student visa/ entry permit?
text:
Question: Do I need a student visa/ entry permit?
Answer: Most non-local students are required to obtain the following for studying in Hong Kong: student visa (if you are from an overseas country) entry permit (if you are from Macao, Taiwan or Mainland China) Please visit here for details.

title: How should I apply for the student visa/ entry permit?
text:
Question: How should I apply for the student visa/ entry permit?
Answer: You can apply through HKUST by lodging your application via HKUST Visa System. The University will act as your sponsor. Please visit here for the application timeline and procedures. For students from Taiwan, Macao or an overseas country, you may choose to file an application on your own (e.g. through the consulate general of your country). You will need to find a sponsor and make your own arrangement to collect the visa label.

title: When should I apply for the student visa/ entry permit?
text:
Question: When should I apply for the student visa/ entry permit?
Answer: Application period: Fall admission: March or soonest possible Spring admission: mid-October or soonest possible For exchange program, PG visiting student program and visiting internship: apply right after you have accepted the admission offer For DBA, EMBA, MBA, HKUST-NYU MSc in Global Finance: respective program offices will advise you of the details separately It is important to submit your application early to ensure that you can obtain the visa/entry permit in time for program commencement. Please visit here for a rough application timeline based on our past experience, for reference purpose only. The processing and approval processes rest with the Hong Kong Immigration Department and situation may vary from case to case.

title: What documents are required for the visa application?
text:
Question: What documents are required for the visa application?
Answer: Please refer to the Checklist of Documents Required for Visa/ Entry Permit Application. You will find more tips in the Visa System. Please make use of the mailing cover when sending the supporting documents by courier to HKUST.

title: What amount should I put as financial proof?
text:
Question: What amount should I put as financial proof?
Answer: It is important to show that you are well covered financially during your studies at HKUST (see sample application form). The Hong Kong Immigration Department requires documentary proof showing your financial standing, e.g. a photocopy of latest bank statement or passbook. If you are awarded a scholarship or a Postgraduate Studentship, please indicate the monthly amount in the application form.

title: Where should I send the visa application documents to?
text:
Question: Where should I send the visa application documents to?
Answer: Please send your documents to the following address with your application number and program: HKUST Fok Ying Tung Graduate School Room 6418 (Lifts 25/26), Academic Building The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology Clear Water Bay, Kowloon Hong Kong Application Number: 6XXXXXXXXXX Program: PhD in XXXX

title: I have sent my paper visa application, what’s next?
text:
Question: I have sent my paper visa application, what’s next?
Answer: Login to check the status in the Visa System. Our office will closely monitor the progress of your application and update status in the system accordingly: "Pending documents from applicant" – please check with the courier service provider about the delivery status and ensure that you have sent the documents to the correct mailing address. If mailing address is correct, please re-check the status in the Visa System a week from now. “Processing by UST/Immigration” – things are in good order and we will further contact you when the application is approved. Otherwise, please write to us and provide us the following information in your message: Name and Application No. Screenshot which could show the delivery status, tracking no., delivery address and recipient’s signature

title: What are the programmes of School of Graduate Studies (GS) and When is the application period for 2026 entry?
text:
Question: What are the programmes of School of Graduate Studies (GS) and When is the application period for 2026 entry?
Answer: ALL applicants are reminded to read the GS’ website carefully before submitting an application. Applications for entry in 2026/27 begin on 01 Oct 2025. The deadlines for applications vary by programme. Please refer to the above website for the deadlines specific to individual programmes.

title: How can I submit my application?
text:
Question: How can I submit my application?
Answer: You can apply for our taught postgraduate programmes via the online application system.

title: Can I apply for more than one programme? Will it affect my chance of success?
text:
Question: Can I apply for more than one programme? Will it affect my chance of success?
Answer: You are required to submit separate applications if you wish to make multiple applications for different programmes of our School. Please also note that multiple application fees should be paid. Your chance of success will not be affected. However, applicants are not encouraged to switch to another programme once an admission offer has been accepted. Please be aware that the deposit fee will not be refunded if you decide to switch to another programme.

title: Can I apply for admission if i am still in my last year of study?
text:
Question: Can I apply for admission if i am still in my last year of study?
Answer: You do not need to graduate from your bachelor’s degree (for master’s degree applications) or master’s degree (for doctoral applications) at the time of application. However, you must obtain the respective degree on or before August 15 of your admission year.You do not need to graduate from your bachelor’s degree (for master’s degree applications) or master’s degree (for doctoral applications) at the time of application. However, you must obtain the respective degree on or before August 15 of your admission year.

title: What kind of supporting documents will be needed for considering my application?
text:
Question: What kind of supporting documents will be needed for considering my application?
Answer: Please click the website for details of the required supporting documents.

title: Do you consider IELTS One Skill Retake score for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement for master's programmes?
text:
Question: Do you consider IELTS One Skill Retake score for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement for master's programmes?
Answer: For master's programmes, IELTS One Skill Retake score is not accepted for fulfilling the minimum English proficiency requirement. The University only considers the IELTS result from an individual test on one single test date.

title: If my TOEFL or IELTS score does not meet the requirement of the programme, could I use other means to demonstrate my English proficiency? Can the CET conducted by universities in China be used to substitute TOEFL or IELTS?
text:
Question: If my TOEFL or IELTS score does not meet the requirement of the programme, could I use other means to demonstrate my English proficiency? Can the CET conducted by universities in China be used to substitute TOEFL or IELTS?
Answer: Generally, applicants are required to submit TOEFL or IELTS scores that meet the programme's requirements as proof of English proficiency. If you have a CET6 score, you may also submit it for the Admissions Committee's consideration. However, acceptance is ultimately subject to the specific policies of each programme.

title: How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted the application?
text:
Question: How do I know that I have successfully paid the application fee and submitted the application?
Answer: Upon successful payment of the application fee and submission of an application, an email notification with application no. will be sent to the applicant.

title: Do I need to attend an interview?
text:
Question: Do I need to attend an interview?
Answer: Shortlisted applicants will be invited to an interview by the Programme Office within 2 to 3 weeks. An applicant who has not been invited to an interview shall not assume that he/she is not being considered for admission.

title: What language will the interview be conducted in?
text:
Question: What language will the interview be conducted in?
Answer: The interview will typically be conducted in English.

title: Do I need to submit reference letters?
text:
Question: Do I need to submit reference letters?
Answer: You can invite your referees to complete the recommendation letters (sample could be downloaded from the online admission system) and to send us by email admission office if they are not convenient to post it. Regarding the reference letter, kindly be alerted that we will not accept reference letters from personal email address(e.g., yahoo, Hotmail, gmail or other similar public system). Please inform your referees to send the soft copy of recommendation letters by their official, company or professional email address. SitemapContact UsPrivacy Policy StatementDisclaimerWeb Accessibility Statement Copyright© 2026 Lingnan University. All rights reserved.

title: What are the differences between Taught Postgraduate programmes and Research programmes?
text:
Question: What are the differences between Taught Postgraduate programmes and Research programmes?
Answer: Taught Postgraduate programmes focus more on coursework. Some may require the completion of a dissertation. For research programmes, the PhD or MPhil degree is awarded to a student who, upon completing an approved programme of study and research, presents a dissertation on the results of his/her research and satisfies the examiners in an oral examination (and other examinations if required) in matters relevant to the subject of the dissertation. For details about our Research Postgraduate programmes, please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/research-postgraduate)

title: When is the application period for admission to Taught Postgraduate programmes?
text:
Question: When is the application period for admission to Taught Postgraduate programmes?
Answer: The September 2027 Entry will start inviting applications from 2 July 2026, and s ome programmes may invite applications for January entry. Please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate) for the most up-to-date information.

title: How can I get information on programmes and admission?
text:
Question: How can I get information on programmes and admission?
Answer: Please visit our Study@PolyU (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate) website for details of our admissions policies, programmes on offer and their entrance requirements, and other admission-related information before you submit an application. Once you have selected a suitable programme, you are encouraged to apply as early as possible since our academic departments will consider applications on a rolling basis until all the places are filled.

title: How do I apply?
text:
Question: How do I apply?
Answer: Applications are received online via eAdmission system (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do) . Please note that our University does not authorize any person, organisation or agency for student recruitment purposes. Interested person should submit your application to us via eAdmission (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do) directly.

title: Who are non-local applicants?
text:
Question: Who are non-local applicants?
Answer: Please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/non-local-applicants-tpg) for the definition of non-local applicants. The levels of tuition fees for local and non-local students are different for some programmes. It is therefore important for applicants to indicate their identity correctly when they submit their application(s).

title: What are the entrance requirements for admission to Taught Postgraduate programmes?
text:
Question: What are the entrance requirements for admission to Taught Postgraduate programmes?
Answer: Please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/admission-requirements-tpg) for details.

title: What are the selection criteria for admission?
text:
Question: What are the selection criteria for admission?
Answer: Applications will be considered on the basis of the information (such as academic achievements, professional qualifications and work experience) provided in the online application form.

title: Can I apply for admission if I do not meet the stipulated entrance requirements?
text:
Question: Can I apply for admission if I do not meet the stipulated entrance requirements?
Answer: Applicants with good calibre but without the relevant qualifications may also be admitted. These applicants must demonstrate sufficient motivation, knowledge and potential to complete the programme successfully.

title: Does PolyU accept UKVI IELTS for applying to taught postgraduate programmes?
text:
Question: Does PolyU accept UKVI IELTS for applying to taught postgraduate programmes?
Answer: The contents and format for both IELTS and IELTS UKVI tests are actually the same. The UKVI number is for reference of the UK Government only. Therefore, you can continue using your IELTS UKVI result to apply for admission to our taught postgraduate programmes.

title: Can I apply for more than one programme at the same time?
text:
Question: Can I apply for more than one programme at the same time?
Answer: Multiple applications are allowed for applicants seeking admission to our taught postgraduate programmes. You can make another application under the same account after you have submitted one application.

title: Can I apply for credit transfer?
text:
Question: Can I apply for credit transfer?
Answer: Applicants who have previously studied at a university, polytechnic or other post-secondary institution and completed subjects that are relevant to their chosen programme may be eligible for credit transfer. Please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/credit-transfer-and-exemption-tpg) for details of the policy governing credit transfer.

title: What can I do if I do not intend to pursue a formal academic award but am interested in some subjects offered under some programmes?
text:
Question: What can I do if I do not intend to pursue a formal academic award but am interested in some subjects offered under some programmes?
Answer: The University has a provision for applicants to enrol on individual subjects which is processed on a semester basis. Please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/subject-based-admission-tpg) for details.

title: What is the meaning of "Mixed-mode" study?
text:
Question: What is the meaning of "Mixed-mode" study?
Answer: Classes are held in both daytime and evening. Students may attend classes in the daytime, evening or a combination of both. Mixed-mode programmes give students an option to engage in a full-time or part-time study load. This provides flexibility for them to determine their own pace of study.

title: Am I eligible for the financial assistance scheme?
text:
Question: Am I eligible for the financial assistance scheme?
Answer: Please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/financial-assistance-and-scholarships-tpg) for details.

title: Are non-local students eligible for financial assistance? Can they take up part-time jobs?
text:
Question: Are non-local students eligible for financial assistance? Can they take up part-time jobs?
Answer: Non-local students are not eligible for financial assistance from both the Government of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region and PolyU. Therefore students have to ensure that they have adequate funds to cover tuition fees, accommodation and other expenses, including medical insurance before accepting the offer to study at PolyU. Notwithstanding the above, non-local students studying a full-time programme at degree level or above with a study period of not less than one academic year will be allowed to take up part-time on-campus employment and summer jobs subject to the conditions as stipulated by the Immigration Department of Hong Kong Special Administrative Region. Please refer to the Student Handbook (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/ar/web/en/for-polyu-students/student-handbook/index.html) for details.

title: Are non-local students eligible for applying for hall residence?
text:
Question: Are non-local students eligible for applying for hall residence?
Answer: Students of Taught Postgraduate programmes are NOT eligible for Students Halls of Residence. They have to secure their own accommodation in Hong Kong and may approach the Non-local Student Services Team (NLSST) for one-stop advisory service. Please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/sao/student-resources-and-support-section/non-local-student-services/) for details. To check out further information on accommodation, please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/sao/student-resources-and-support-section/non-local-student-services/living-and-working-in-hong-kong/off-campus-accommodation-information/) Before Application Submission

title: How much is the application fee?
text:
Question: How much is the application fee?
Answer: The application fee for admission to taught postgraduate programmes for 2027/28 is HK$600, except for Master of Business Administration (MBA) and doctorate programmes of Faculty of Business. The application fee for MBA is $800, while the fee of doctorate programmes of Faculty of Business is $1,200 for 2027/28 intake cohort. The fee is non-refundable and non-transferrable. You must settle the application fee upon submission of your online application, otherwise, your application will not be further processed. Please note that other than the specified application fee, you are not required to pay any money to any persons in connection with your application.

title: What should I do if my results are not yet available when I submit my application?
text:
Question: What should I do if my results are not yet available when I submit my application?
Answer: If you have not yet received your results/transcript(s)/certificate(s) when you apply, please indicate in your application the date on which they will be available. You should update your results and upload your final official transcript(s)/certificate(s) to your application on or before the date which you have specified.

title: Should I submit additional/supporting documents?
text:
Question: Should I submit additional/supporting documents?
Answer: You are required to upload your identity document transcript(s)/certificate(s) in support of your qualifications and other documents (including official English translation if your documents are not in English) as specifically required for your programme choices when you submit your application. Please check the details at our Study@PolyU (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate) website (select “Applying to PolyU” at the top menu bar > click the “Additional Documents Required” section). Failure to complete the online application form properly and failure to submit the required documents may affect your chances of admission. You need not submit more documents than required as non-required documents will not be considered by departments. The academic department concerned may, in the selection process, approach you for clarification of your qualifications.

title: Should I submit documents to programme host departments directly?
text:
Question: Should I submit documents to programme host departments directly?
Answer: No. Please submit them together with your online application form, unless specified by individual programme host department.

title: What should I do if my academic documents are not in English?
text:
Question: What should I do if my academic documents are not in English?
Answer: Please provide officially translated copies of the documents in addition to copies of the original documents; otherwise your application may not be considered.

title: Is IELTS or TOEFL compulsory for admission?
text:
Question: Is IELTS or TOEFL compulsory for admission?
Answer: Generally students are expected to fulfil English Language Requirement (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/admission-requirements-tpg) , English language qualifications other than IELTS and TOEFL will be considered individually on their own merits by the departments concerned. Applicants may be required to attend interviews or tests to further demonstrate their language proficiency.

title: What should I do if I want to apply for credit transfer?
text:
Question: What should I do if I want to apply for credit transfer?
Answer: You are not required to specify this when you submit your application. The departments concerned will decide if credit transfer can be granted based on your qualifications. The total credits you are required to study will be shown on your Notice of Offer if you are given a study place.

title: Can I change my programme choice after submission?
text:
Question: Can I change my programme choice after submission?
Answer: You are not allowed to change your programme choice after your application has been submitted. However, you may submit another application under the same account after you have submitted one application.

title: Can I apply for re-admission?
text:
Question: Can I apply for re-admission?
Answer: If you have withdrawn from a programme of study at PolyU without completing the withdrawal procedures or have been required to withdraw in the previous academic year, you will not be considered for re-admission to the same programme in this academic year. Any fees paid will not be refunded. You are however still eligible to apply for admission to other programmes.

title: How will PolyU use the information collected from my application?
text:
Question: How will PolyU use the information collected from my application?
Answer: All information we collect from your application form will only be used for processing your application for admission to our programmes and for statistical and research purposes. Please refer to the Personal Information Collection Statement (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/ar/pics/) of PolyU for details. After Application Submission

title: How can I settle my application fee?
text:
Question: How can I settle my application fee?
Answer: You will see a payment reminder page after you have submitted your application. You can go directly to the "Payment Page" to settle your application fee immediately. Alternatively, you can log into your eAdmission account before the payment deadline to settle the fee at a later stage. For details of the payment methods, please click here (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/fo/students/fee-and-payment/payment-methods-for-applicants/) It normally takes 2 working days to transfer the payment record to PolyU, but it takes 5 working days if you pay via Flywire. Please make sure that your application fee is properly settled before the specified payment deadline. Otherwise, your application will not be further processed. We will accordingly cancel your application when the payment status is "Overdue". (Please log into your eAdmission account to check whether the payment status has been updated to "Paid" after you have settled the fee.)

title: Is the application fee refundable?
text:
Question: Is the application fee refundable?
Answer: The application fee is non-refundable and non-transferrable.

title: Where can I find my application number?
text:
Question: Where can I find my application number?
Answer: An application number will be assigned to you and shown on your online application once you have successfully submitted your application.

title: What should I do if I want to submit additional/supporting documents after application submission?
text:
Question: What should I do if I want to submit additional/supporting documents after application submission?
Answer: You are allowed to submit further documents after application submission, except if you are being considered for an offer. Please scan the documents and log into your eAdmission account for the upload. You may wish to check the supporting documents specifically required by individual programmes at our Study@PolyU (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate) website (select “Applying to PolyU” at the top menu bar > click the “Additional Documents Required” section). Please, however, note that you need not submit more documents than required as non-required documents will not be considered by departments.

title: How can I verify my application data?
text:
Question: How can I verify my application data?
Answer: Please log into your eAdmission account to verify your application data. If you cannot find your application data, please contact us (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/enquiry) immediately (please quote your application number in all your correspondences with PolyU). As the information you entered in the online application form will form the basis on which your application will be considered, you should make sure that it is complete and accurate. Otherwise, your chances of admission may be affected, and any offer made will also be withdrawn if it is based on incomplete and inaccurate application data.

title: How can I update my application data?
text:
Question: How can I update my application data?
Answer: Most of the data which you have entered, except for the programme choice(s), can be changed after your application has been submitted but before you are being considered for an offer. If you have indicated that your results are not yet available by filling out the "Update Result Date", you can update your results on or before the date specified. As the information you entered in the online application form will form the basis on which your application will be considered, you should make sure that it is complete and accurate. Otherwise, your chances of admission may be affected, and any offer made will also be withdrawn if it is based on incomplete and inaccurate application data.

title: How can I know my application result?
text:
Question: How can I know my application result?
Answer: If you are given an offer or a conditional offer, you will be informed via email/SMS. You may also log into your eAdmission account from time to time to check the progress of your application. If there is no news by early September (for September entry) or mid-January (for January entry), you can assume that your application is unsuccessful. No separate notification will be sent for unsuccessful cases. Upon Receiving Offer

title: What should I do to accept an offer or a conditional offer?
text:
Question: What should I do to accept an offer or a conditional offer?
Answer: Please indicate your acceptance and settle the registration fee or tuition fee before the "last day for payment" displayed in the Notice of Offer, and register on the programme registration date as indicated on the Notice of Offer. (For conditional offer, you need to complete the procedures for meeting the conditions stated in the Notice of Offer beforehand.)

title: How can I complete the meet condition(s) procedure?
text:
Question: How can I complete the meet condition(s) procedure?
Answer: For meeting condition(s), please return to the Online Notice of Offer page via eAdmission to complete the procedures for "Meeting Conditions" when you have acquired the relevant qualifications on or before the "Meet Condition Date".

title: What should I do to accept another offer from PolyU after I have paid the tuition fees for an earlier offer?
text:
Question: What should I do to accept another offer from PolyU after I have paid the tuition fees for an earlier offer?
Answer: Please contact us (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/enquiry) to transfer the fees already paid for the earlier offer to the other offer. Please remember to provide application number of both offers in your request. This arrangement only applies if both programmes lead to PolyU awards.

title: Can I enrol on two programmes at the same time?
text:
Question: Can I enrol on two programmes at the same time?
Answer: Students are not allowed to enrol concurrently on two full-time/sandwich programmes whether or not one of the programmes is offered by another institution. Except for programmes which do not lead to any formal award, students are not allowed to enrol concurrently on a full-time/sandwich programme and a part-time programme, or more than one part-time programmes, including those offered by another institution without any approval from the Head of your academic Department.

title: Is the tuition fee paid refundable?
text:
Question: Is the tuition fee paid refundable?
Answer: All fees paid will not be refunded except in the case of programme cancellation.

title: When will the offer of a programme marked with "subject to approval" be confirmed?
text:
Question: When will the offer of a programme marked with "subject to approval" be confirmed?
Answer: You can assume that the offer is confirmed unless you are separately notified of its non-approval.

title: What if the programme I have applied for is cancelled?
text:
Question: What if the programme I have applied for is cancelled?
Answer: In that case, both the tuition fee and application fee paid will be refunded to you.

title: When should I submit my student visa application?
text:
Question: When should I submit my student visa application?
Answer: It will normally take 8-10 weeks for the Immigration Department of HKSAR to process your application, therefore, you should complete and return the student visa application form with all the required documents to us by express post as soon as possible after you have accepted our offer. For details, please refer to offer confirmation section of the Notice of Offer in eAdmission.

title: Do I need to provide proof of financial means with my student visa application?
text:
Question: Do I need to provide proof of financial means with my student visa application?
Answer: The Hong Kong Immigration Department requires all non-local students to provide proof of their ability to pay tuition fees and living expenses upon submission of their student visa applications. This proof can take the form of personal or family bank letters/bank statements.

title: How can I check the status of my student visa application?
text:
Question: How can I check the status of my student visa application?
Answer: You can check the latest status of your student visa application via eAdmission (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do) from time to time.

title: When can I receive my e-Visa?
text:
Question: When can I receive my e-Visa?
Answer: Once your visa application is approved, you will receive an email notification on how to download your e-Visa from the Hong Kong Immigration Department’s website. However, if you are given a conditional offer, you will only receive the email notification after you have met the condition(s) of your admission offer.

title: How should I submit my original test score report (i.e., GMAT, GRE, IETLS or TOEFL)?
text:
Question: How should I submit my original test score report (i.e., GMAT, GRE, IETLS or TOEFL)?
Answer: If your score reports can be verified online, you should inform the relevant test/ examination provider to release the right to PolyU to verify your results online. PolyU's institutional code is CB8 for GMAT, 3404 for GRE and 0732 for TOEFL. IETLS score report could be verified online without an access code. You are therefore not required to request IELTS to send us the hard copy report unless you are asked to do so.

title: How can I check the status of qualification verification?
text:
Question: How can I check the status of qualification verification?
Answer: You can check the latest status of qualification verification via eAdmission (https://www38.polyu.edu.hk/eAdmission/index.do) from time to time.

title: How to submit required documents for qualification verification?
text:
Question: How to submit required documents for qualification verification?
Answer: Please refer to the Guidelines on Submission of Required Documents for Qualification Verification ( link (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/aradm/tpg/guidelines_qualification_verification.pdf) ) for details. Please note that all qualifications (except those obtained from PolyU and CityU) mentioned in your application should be submitted for verifications. Failure to do so or any discrepancies found may lead to nullification of your offer and registration at the University. Fees paid will not be refunded. You can only proceed with online programme registration after completion of qualification verification and approval of student visa application (if applicable). Fees / Financial Assistance (https://www.polyu.edu.hk/study/pg/taught-postgraduate/general-information-tpg)
